# TE’Oga STORY BIBLE • MASTER REFERENCE (Innocent Edition)
Version 1.1 • Addendum Sections (§12–§14)

Scope: canonical facts only. Practical, neutral, in-world manual voice. No twists, no foreshadowing.

---

## §12 • Political Case Studies (one per capital)

> Format per city: governance, power blocs, recurrent disputes, leverage points, cross-border posture, field guidance.

### 12.1 Grindheim (Underface)
**Governance.** Guild-municipal council chaired by the **Ember Crown** Warden. Two standing seats for the Veinwrights’ Conclave and Stonebinders.  
**Power blocs.**  
- Ember Crown (foundry guild; controls furnace allotments).  
- Stonebinders (Xilcore craft-law; inspectors embedded in projects).  
- Salvagers’ Ordinate (Seeri-aligned reuse syndicate).  
- Oathkeepers tribunal (Blemo contract court; small militia).  
**Recurrent disputes.** Furnace rations during drought; bridge-rights over the Blackgate Gorge; scrap-claim conflicts between Salvagers and private yards.  
**Leverage points.** Furnace allotment ledgers; rivergate tariffs; emergency “Halt Orders” the Conclave can issue on mass-work.  
**Cross-border posture.** Skeptical of Crown fashion and Quietist preaching. Trades iron bar for Crown salts and medica glass.  
**Field guidance.** Wear Conclave blue when brasting in public. Never Unline a working canal wall without a signed Warden writ. Expect crowds to gather fast; post a Bell-runner before any large lift.

---

### 12.2 Cinderfell (Underface)
**Governance.** Hazard-city near Bore rim; ruled by a Tri-warden of **Alloy Collegium**, **Hazard Crews**, **Surveyors’ Chain**.  
**Power blocs.**  
- Hazard Crews (public safety; Harrow response).  
- Alloy Collegium (experimental foundries, license labs).  
- Couriers’ Bell (the only sanctioned cross-Curtain runners here).  
**Recurrent disputes.** Lab safety vs production quotas; Courier exclusivity; salvage rights after light-surges.  
**Leverage points.** Access to the Bore Rim; issuance of hazard-stipends; right to close streets during drills.  
**Cross-border posture.** Pragmatic; sells alloy recipes for Crown salts and lens-sand.  
**Field guidance.** Carry heat-shade cloaks (Shade Steps 8–12 common). All work logged at the Hazard House. If the light goes violet, drop tools and clear to second lines.

---

### 12.3 Hammerpost (Underface)
**Governance.** Military-logistics city at canyon crossroads; Marshal-Magistrate answers to the Triad Compact.  
**Power blocs.**  
- Bridgewright Corps (state-trained brast wardens).  
- Rivet Quarter Syndics (teamsters and cartwrights).  
- Final Measures (Seeri funerary order; controls canyon ossuaries).  
**Recurrent disputes.** Toll hikes; bridge weight-limits; funerary route priority after collapses.  
**Leverage points.** Toll exemptions; access to the Gorgeways; burial allocations.  
**Cross-border posture.** Hard-nosed; prefers iron-for-coin, suspicious of “airy” Crown goods.  
**Field guidance.** Present stamped load sheets. Expect inspection of hammers and wedges. Bridgewright Corps will demand a demonstration brast before approving foreign wardens.

---

### 12.4 Strathmoor (Outer Crown)
**Governance.** Collegium Council of Glass Houses; First Provost rotates by seniority.  
**Power blocs.**  
- Collegium of Flows (research and training; sets veld standards).  
- White Kilns (Blemo-aligned purity guild; certifies medical glass).  
- Roundhouse Medica (Leesa hospitals; strong public trust).  
**Recurrent disputes.** Research secrecy vs public safety; export quotas for flawless panes; medica access to proprietary formulas.  
**Leverage points.** Kiln schedules; vellum publication licenses; access to apprentice lists.  
**Cross-border posture.** Courteous; trades lenses and medical ware for Underface alloy. Tolerates Quietists so long as they don’t disrupt clinics.  
**Field guidance.** Announce veld intent aloud before beginning. No sand-throwing to cool without a sandmaster present. Expect apprentices to observe and take notes.

---

### 12.5 Aurelion (Outer Crown)
**Governance.** Sun-lens farms under a civic Senate; Oath Ledgers kept central.  
**Power blocs.**  
- Oathkeepers’ Registry (Blemo; contracts, bonds, marriage writs).  
- Lens-Farm Syndics (landholders; weather-rights).  
- Mercers’ Table (trade court; regulates weights, coin purity).  
**Recurrent disputes.** False-oath prosecutions; light-rights between farms; coinage shortages during storm-season.  
**Leverage points.** Oath renewal festivals; Senate decrees on lens angles; mercantile embargoes.  
**Cross-border posture.** Active diplomacy with Grindheim; hosts the largest Seam Market.  
**Field guidance.** Keep your ledger straight; expect random bond checks. Small-load veld can manage crowds; ask an Oathkeeper to announce it for you.

---

### 12.6 Veslara (Outer Crown)
**Governance.** Cliff-city; Elder Council weighted toward Leesa medica and Joiner guilds.  
**Power blocs.**  
- Roundhouse Medica (public health; Vein care).  
- Joiners’ Circle (masonry and civic union law).  
- Wind-Organ Conservators (cultural-religious stewards; Leesa rites).  
**Recurrent disputes.** Clinic triage in plague years; union protections vs emergency works; preservation of wind organs vs expansion.  
**Leverage points.** Clinic access; union endorsements; ritual schedules.  
**Cross-border posture.** Bridge-building with Hammerpost; exchanges medica exchanges for stone engineers.  
**Field guidance.** Announce your rest cycles; Veslara enforces Warden rest. Never interrupt an organ rite; it doubles as a crowd-safety signal.

---

## §13 • Field Kits & Tool Glossaries

> Standardized lists for continuity. Swap or add by locale, but core items remain consistent unless stated otherwise.

### 13.1 Veinwright (Brast) Kit — Underface standard
- **Hammers:** face, cross-peen, rounding (marked 1–3 by weight).  
- **Wedges & Dogs:** three sizes, tempered; chalked with hex marks.  
- **Tongs:** flat, wolf-jaw, pick.  
- **Feeler Irons:** thin rods to test give across joints.  
- **Heat Bell Cards:** eight-step bell disks for common alloys.  
- **Quench Vessels:** oil can, water bucket, brine jar (color-coded cords).  
- **Line Chalk:** iron-red; Conclave-approved mix.  
- **Ledger & Seal:** job log, blue Conclave seal-stamp.  
- **Safety:** leather hood, forearm bracers, bell whistle.  
- **Ritual:** Xilcore tool-string (optional, common).

**Usage notes.** Face hammer for setting; peen for drawing line; rounding for easing Harrow backwash. Bell cards must be checked against local altitude and draught.

---

### 13.2 Veinwright (Veld) Kit — Crown standard
- **Blowpipes:** short, long; brass mouth-guards.  
- **Punty Rods:** detachable tips; color dots for heat memory.  
- **Marvers & Paddles:** polished stone, wet cedar.  
- **Blocks & Jacks:** three bowl sizes; wooden jacks for shaping.  
- **Anneal Sand & Cradles:** graded sand (Shade Steps 3–9), cooling cradles on wheeled racks.  
- **Shade Gauge:** 12-step viewing lens; cloth hoods for glare.  
- **Thread-Clamps:** to “hold the line” while turning.  
- **Line Ink:** pale gold; leaves a faint tracery on cooled glass.  
- **Safety:** sleeve guards, breath-veil, water ladle, burn salve.  
- **Ledger & Seal:** kiln schedule, White Kiln purity stamps.

**Usage notes.** Announce shade step aloud; apprentices repeat. No quenching panes over Shade 7 without a sandmaster present.

---

### 13.3 Surveyors’ Chain Kit
- **Chains:** 10-chain lengths with lead tags every fifth chain.  
- **Plumb Lines & Levels:** oil bubble vials; spare corks.  
- **Sight Boards:** folding, with black/white chevrons.  
- **Rift Probes:** weighted cane rods; red flags for voids.  
- **Map Folio:** oilskin; charcoal sticks, gum erasers.  
- **Signal Flags:** red halt, blue clear, yellow caution.  
- **Bell Codes:** pocket chart for two-runner teams.

---

### 13.4 Couriers’ Bell Kit
- **Runner Satchel:** triple-sleeved, wax-sealed pockets.  
- **Bell Pair:** tuned handbells for line-of-sight code.  
- **Writ Wallet:** Seam writ, temple safe-passage, guild letters.  
- **Shoes:** ridge-grip soles; spare laces.  
- **Cloak:** Curtain-weight, reversible (grey/blue).  
- **Rations & Salts:** dried fruit, salt tabs, willow bark.  
- **Knife & Pin:** rope knife; Conclave pin visible.

---

### 13.5 Hazard Crew Kit (Cinderfell pattern)
- **Heat Cloaks:** Shade 10–12 rated.  
- **Hook Poles:** slag pullers; glass snaggers.  
- **Water Lines:** quick-couplers to cistern carts.  
- **Breath Buckets:** wet cloths; vinegar flasks.  
- **Stretcher & Splints:** two-man carry; bolt-on wheels.  
- **Ward Ropes:** waist ties with quick-release.  
- **Siren Cones:** hand sirens; violet and amber filters.

---

### 13.6 Medica Vein-Care Kit (Veslara)
- **Fray Salves:** willow-oil, comfrey, biter’s balm.  
- **Cooling Stones:** river rounds for veld burns.  
- **Pulse Bands:** cloth bands to monitor strain signs (color shift to pale when overworked).  
- **Needles & Thread:** bone and copper; boiled linen.  
- **Leesa Round Tokens:** for clinic priority.  
- **Quiet Rooms:** portable screens; sand bowls for breath-count work.

---

### 13.7 Auditor Inspection Pack
- **Writ Case:** halt orders, seizure slips, Conclave authorization.  
- **Measuring Rod:** stamped spans, hidden plumb.  
- **Ink & Seals:** blue Conclave, city red, guild black.  
- **Witness Ledger:** blank pages tied with red cord; requires two marks per entry.  
- **Badge:** enamel blue with chain/quill; visible at all times.  
- **Witness Pins:** temporary pins for civilian observers.

---

### 13.8 Oathkeeper Militia Kit (Aurelion)
- **Short Batons:** for crowd-spacing; hex-knurled.  
- **Bond Cords:** numbered ties for detainees; log card.  
- **Shield Boards:** light wood with iron rim; drop sigil.  
- **Horn Pair:** low and high tone; oath-breach call.  
- **Writ Folders:** arrest, bond, mediation drafts.

---

### 13.9 Seam Market Trader Kit
- **Stall Cloths:** neutral grey; Conclave mark visible.  
- **Weights:** Crown and Underface sets; stamped by Mercers’ Table.  
- **Indicator Cards:** heat, shade, load tolerances for wares.  
- **Lockbox:** two-key system; one key held by partner.  
- **Safe-Exit Map:** updated weekly; Curtain shifts.

---

### 13.10 Bore-Rim Expedition Kit (restricted)
- **Goggles:** glare-cut, violet filter.  
- **Anchors & Lines:** glass-bite tips; slag screws.  
- **Heat Drums:** water bladders; slow drip for paths.  
- **Violet Tracer:** chalk that darkens near light-surges.  
- **Recall Flag:** yellow chevron; “fall back to last mark.”

---

### 13.11 Tool Glossary (selected)
- **Cleave-iron:** narrow wedge for initiating a controlled Unline.  
- **Bridge-pin:** temporary load-sharing pin; painted blue.  
- **Shade hood:** cloth cowl for veld work; notch indicates step.  
- **Bell cards:** rotating discs with alloy or sand mixtures tied to bell counts.  
- **Line ink:** visible on cooled glass; fades on iron.  
- **Fray band:** wrist cloth that pales when personal threft spikes.

---

## §14 • Expanded Dossiers & Population Culture

### 14A. Expanded Character Dossiers (action contexts)

#### Chloen of Grindheim
**Function.** Surveyor-scribe, **Surveyors’ Chain**.  
**Competencies.** Cleavepoint mapping, rift probing, bell-code coordination, ledger accuracy under duress.  
**Action contexts.**  
- **Rift assessment.** Establishes safe spans across unstable glass-scar ground; sets red-flag arrays.  
- **Crowd guidance.** Uses flag/bell to create breathing lanes during Harrow.  
- **Seam documentation.** Maintains neutral maps used by both hemispheres; trusted by Conclave.  
**Default kit.** Sight board, chain, flag set, oilskin folio, blue Conclave ribbon.  
**Risk posture.** Low to moderate; refuses to enter zones beyond second flag line.  
**Interpersonal.** Dry humor, debt-averse, prefers written oaths.  
**Known limits.** Weak swimmer; dislikes cliff work.  
**Public reputation.** “The ledger that keeps bridges up.”

---

#### Kylar of Strathmoor
**Function.** Oathkeeper escort, **Aurelion Registry** detachment stationed in Strathmoor.  
**Competencies.** Crowd spacing, nonlethal baton work, bond law, small-load brast (shock-line dispersal).  
**Action contexts.**  
- **Market disputes.** Steps between factions, sets perimeters, calls high horn for mediators.  
- **Escort duty.** Moves field teams through tense districts; posts two-cord buffer.  
- **Oath breach.** Recognizes forged ledgers; arrests cleanly with witnesses logged.  
**Default kit.** Short batons, shield board, horn pair, bond cords, writ folder.  
**Risk posture.** Moderate; holds line until medica or Conclave arrive.  
**Interpersonal.** Formal, literal; relents for elders and apprentices.  
**Known limits.** Over-trusting of stamped papers; needs Chloen’s scrutiny.  
**Public reputation.** “The rule that doesn’t bruise.”

---

### 14B. Population Culture Dossiers (crowd behavior, customs, norms)

> Use these to write lived-in scenes. They’re the “background logic” readers will feel even when not stated.

#### Underface (Tempered Realms) – Grindheim, Cinderfell, Hammerpost
**Daily rhythm.** Bell-cycles start early; work breaks at Second and Fifth Bell; taverns fill after Seventh.  
**Street foods.** Skillet breads, iron-pot stews, brine pickles, slag-sugar crisps.  
**Etiquette.** Tools are personal; never touch another’s hammer. Eye contact brief; nod once for thanks.  
**Crowd dynamics.** Crowds condense toward the strong voice; respond to whistles and hammer-on-iron more than spoken orders.  
**Superstitions.** Three taps on iron before a first pour; coins on new bridges for luck.  
**Festivals.** Forge-Round (first pour), Return to Soil (Seeri thanks), Bridge Naming days.  
**Attitudes to law.** Respect for clear rules; loathing for “paper puzzles.”  
**Common slang.**  
- “Hold your line.” Stay steady.  
- “You’re all bell, no heat.” All talk.  
- “Lay a bridge.” Make amends.

---

#### Outer Crown (Breathwright Domains) – Strathmoor, Aurelion, Veslara
**Daily rhythm.** Shade Steps pace the day; long midday rest in high glare.  
**Street foods.** Flatbreads, citrus salts, chilled sweets, seed brittle.  
**Etiquette.** Announce intent; hands visible when speaking; share water at thresholds.  
**Crowd dynamics.** Crowds part for the Shade Gauge; respond to chimes and hand signs.  
**Superstitions.** Never praise flawless glass; leave a bubble “for luck.”  
**Festivals.** Clearwake (first flawless pane), Joining Day (amnesties), Oath-Renewal.  
**Attitudes to law.** Contract-first culture; mercy expected when truth is plain.  
**Common slang.**  
- “Mind your shade.” Know your limits.  
- “Too clear by half.” Naive, or suspiciously perfect.  
- “Give it breath.” Allow time.

---

#### Seam Markets (the Curtain crossings)
**Composition.** Mixed crews; Conclave blue visible; armed peace by custom.  
**Conduct.** Prices in Marks or Crowns at seller’s discretion; weights checked by Mercers’ Table booth.  
**Signals.** Three-bell roll = clear path for hazard crew. Red flag strung above aisle = halt trade.  
**Risks.** Pickpockets, rumor stampedes, Shatterhand sabotage.  
**Norms.** No temple proselytizing; no militia colors; disputes to Witness Table.

---

#### Crowd Management Patterns (for Harrow scenes)
- **Underface rule of six.** One warden for each six carts; bell runner every third corner.  
- **Crown spiral.** Turn crowds clockwise around an incident, never into oncoming glare.  
- **Shared signal.** Raised open palm = stop; crossed wrists = route blocked; two fingers forward = move slow.

---

#### Family & Apprenticeship
**Underface.** Children apprenticed by Second Round of schooling; surnames often replaced by guild marks in public matters.  
**Crown.** Apprenticeship chosen after Trial Week at a House; family names retained on oaths.  
**Marriage & bonds.** Oaths witnessed at temple or by Oathkeepers; dowries are practical (tools, shop shares).  
**Funerary.** Underface returns tools to guild, ashes to Seeri beds; Crown wraps in white cloth, inters with a small pane etched with name and first craft.

---

#### Speech & Address (safe defaults)
- **Craft peers:** “wright,” “warden,” “assessor,” “master,” “apprentice.”  
- **Civic:** “provost,” “marshal,” “elder.”  
- **Religious:** “keeper” (Blemo), “round-mother/father” (Leesa), “binder” (Xilcore), “reader of ends” (Seeri).  
- **Common honorifics:** “Goodhand,” “Goodmind.”

---

#### Rumor Ecology
- **Underface.** News rides with ore wagons and tavern singers; Conclave postings read evenings.  
- **Crown.** News broadsheets at dawn; Collegium boards in afternoons; chime-gossip at clinics.  
- **Seam.** Nothing is true until Mercers stamp the weight.

---

#### Bias & Stereotypes (write with care; these create friction)
- Underface about Crown: “Fine glass, thin skin.”  
- Crown about Underface: “Strong hands, blunt minds.”  
- Both about Seam Traders: “Quick hands, quicker ledgers.”  
These biases soften fast in mixed crews who work a shared job.

---

### 14C. Crowd Archetypes (for scene texture)
- **Bell-Runner (youth).** Knows alleys; loyal to coin and praise.  
- **Kiln-Mother (elder).** Mediates disputes with two words and a stare.  
- **Scrapman (Seeri).** Spots value in wreckage; respected, never fully trusted.  
- **Salt-Brother (Aurelion).** Contract hawk; fair if watched.  
- **Rope-Girl (Hammerpost).** Bridge aide; fearless on heights.  
- **Pane-Singer (Strathmoor).** Times cooling with chant; superstitious about bubbles.  
- **Gutter-Priest (any).** Gives last rites; collects folk stories.

---

### 14D. Public Documents & Seals (props you can show)
- **Seam Writ** (blue): travel through the Curtain.  
- **Halt Order** (Conclave): stops a job; blue seal, red countersign.  
- **Oath Ledger Leaf** (Blemo): bond terms; tear-off slip for each party.  
- **Clinic Token** (Leesa): round green chit with line mark.  
- **Salvage Claim** (Seeri): thorn stamp, site sketch attached.

---

### 14E. Quick Safety Mnemonics (in-world sayings)
- “**Three bells to cool, two to speak, one to strike.**”  
- “**Mark the line or mark the grave.**”  
- “**No breath before the bridge.**”  
- “**If the shade goes violet, you don’t.**”

---

- §16 Map Insets: standard street plan symbols for each capital; rift sign systems.  
- §17 Training Progressions: competencies by rank for brast and veld practitioners.

If you want, I’ll continue building those sections in the same neutral, usable style.

# TE’Oga STORY BIBLE • MASTER REFERENCE (Innocent Edition)  
Version 1.2 • Addendum §§15–18

## §15 • Case Materials (exemplar documents)

> Use these as in-world props or to standardize procedure language. Replace bracketed text with specifics.

### 15.1 Conclave HALT ORDER (Mass-Work Suspension)
- **Header:** Veinwrights’ Conclave • Grindheim Chapter  
- **Writ No.:** [GW-612-347] • **Date:** [Round 612 AS, Turn 7, Hand 3]  
- **Site:** [Blackgate Gorge, West Span]  
- **Cause:** Probable **Harrow Risk** due to unlogged **Unline** on pier 3.  
- **Order:** All brast/veld suspended. Remove public within 2 chains.  
- **Custody:** Tools locked on site; keys held by Conclave Assessor.  
- **Compliance Window:** One Bell.  
- **Seals:** Blue Conclave; City Red; Guild Black.  
- **Witness Lines:** [Name, mark] • [Name, mark]

**Back page:** checklist for reopening (ledger review, load test, bell count, fray check of wardens).

---

### 15.2 Oathkeepers’ BOND LEDGER LEAF (Aurelion Registry)
- **Parties:** [Master] and [Client]  
- **Object:** [Span repair / Pane replacement / Escort]  
- **Term:** [Hands / Turns] • **Penalty:** [Marks / Crowns]  
- **Escape Clause:** Force majeure during Curtain storms.  
- **Countersign:** Keeper of Bonds  
- **Receipt Slip:** tear-off for each party with bond number.

---

### 15.3 Clinic TRIAGE SHEET (Veslara Roundhouse Medica)
- **Arrival Time:** [Bell] • **Escort:** [Unit]  
- **Craft Exposure:** brast / veld / neither  
- **Fray Signs:** none / mild / etched / tremor  
- **Cooling/Heating Injury:** shade [0–12] • bell count [ ]  
- **Treatment Path:** Quiet room • Salves • Bridge rest • Oath consult  
- **Return-to-Work:** after [Hands] with bands; **Restrictions:** [Load/Heat limits]  
- **Keeper Mark:** [Leesa round sigil]

---

### 15.4 Seam Writ (Curtain Crossing)
- **Bearer:** [Name] • **Crew:** [Names]  
- **Authority:** Conclave + Mercers’ Table  
- **Goods:** [List] • **Weights:** [By set]  
- **Valid Routes:** [Seam Market A ↔ Market B]  
- **Storm Clause:** Writ suspended during violet-flag conditions.  
- **Void If:** militia colors displayed; proselytizing; unlogged mass-work.

---

### 15.5 Salvage Claim (Seeri Cloister)
- **Find Site:** [Rift / Collapse] • sketch attached  
- **Claimant:** [Name] • **Assistants:** [Names]  
- **Type:** metal / glass / timber / tool / ledger  
- **Hazard:** heat / gas / fall • mitigations noted  
- **Cleric Mark:** thorn spiral • **Expiry:** 1 Turn

---

## §16 • Map Insets & Symbol Standards

> Apply the same symbols on city plans, site diagrams, and expedition sketches.

### 16.1 Line Symbols
- **Primary road:** double solid line.  
- **Gorgeway/Bridge:** bold line with tick marks at spans.  
- **Seam route:** dashed line with small triangles (wind).  
- **Heat main (see §18):** thick red line with hollow circles at intervals.  
- **Glass main (cooling channel):** thin gold line, small squares.

### 16.2 Sites & Structures
- **Conclave House:** blue square with quill.  
- **Temple:** god sigil inside circle (hex/drop/ring/thorn).  
- **Clinic (Roundhouse):** green ring.  
- **Kiln/Furnace:** black square with chimney.  
- **Hazard Station:** red triangle.  
- **Auditor Office:** blue hexagon.  
- **Market:** open square grid.

### 16.3 Terrain & Hazards
- **Rift/Chasm:** heavy black jag line; width annotated in **Fields**.  
- **Glass-scar:** pale gold wash with crackle.  
- **Slag flow:** brown wash with arrows.  
- **Curtain shear:** grey hatching with wind arrows.  
- **Bore glare zone:** violet stipple.

### 16.4 Flags & Codes (street-level)
- **Red flag:** halt all work/trade.  
- **Blue flag:** Conclave work in progress.  
- **Yellow chevron:** fall back to last mark.  
- **Bell codes:**  
  - Single long: clear the span.  
  - Two short: brace load.  
  - Three fast: Harrow imminent.  
  - Rolling bell: hazard crew right-of-way.

### 16.5 Sample Insets (usage notes)
- **Grindheim: Blackgate Sector map** shows stacked terraces, two bridges, heat main junction, Conclave yard, slag drains.  
- **Strathmoor: Kiln Quarter plan** shows lens farms, shade alleys, cooling courts, glass mains.  
- **Seam Market plan** shows witness table, weights booth, neutral stalls, storm exits.

---

## §17 • Training Progressions (competency ladders)

> Ranks: **Apprentice → Journeyman → Warden → Master → Assessor** (where applicable). All times given in **Hands** and **Turns**; adapt to local practice.

### 17.1 Veinwright (Brast)
- **Apprentice (6–12 Turns):** hammer control, load math, bell cards, basic Harrow drill. Safe limit: 1 Field span repairs under Warden.  
- **Journeyman (12–24 Turns):** pier resets, controlled Unline, crowd-steady protocols. Safe limit: 2 Field projects; supervise 2 apprentices.  
- **Warden (by examination):** bridge lifts, mass-work logging, halt order authority on site. Safe limit: 3 Field jobs; issue blue flag.  
- **Master:** complex spans, multi-crew coordination, Conclave liaison.  
- **Assessor:** audits, certifies, writes halt orders.

**Health rails:** Fray bands required; two Bells rest after any Harrow event; clinic visit if bands pale twice in a Hand.

---

### 17.2 Veinwright (Veld)
- **Apprentice:** shade gauge, blowpipe handling, basic anneal, crowd spacing by hand-sign.  
- **Journeyman:** large panes, mold work, dual cooling lanes, ledger timing.  
- **Warden:** kiln schedules, emergency cool, purity liaison (White Kilns).  
- **Master:** structural glass, clinic glass, field cool during fires.  
- **Assessor:** certifies Houses, investigates pane failures.

**Health rails:** Breath-veil for Shade 6+; no solo work over Shade 7; Quiet room after veld burns.

---

### 17.3 Surveyors’ Chain
- **Apprentice:** chain work, plumb/level, basic map ink.  
- **Journeyman:** rift probing, bell-runner control, hazard flags.  
- **Warden:** city sector maps, Conclave overlay integration.  
- **Master:** cross-Curtain charts, storm model notes.

---

### 17.4 Hazard Crews (Cinderfell)
- **Runner:** siren codes, ward ropes, stretcher drills.  
- **Handler:** heat cloaks, hook pole technique, cistern coupling.  
- **Lead:** triage triage, perimeter control, violet-light retreat call.

---

### 17.5 Oathkeepers (Aurelion)
- **Deputy:** bond law basics, baton spacing, witness logging.  
- **Officer:** riot lines with small-load brast, writ execution, mediation.  
- **Registrar:** ledger audits, false-oath detection.

---

### 17.6 Conclave Auditors
- **Inspector:** document checks, tool seizure, on-site photosketches.  
- **Assessor:** full halt authority, cross-border writs, inquiry convening.

---

## §18 • The Thermal Mains (“Heat Pipes”)

> Public canon: every capital and its satellites are threaded by an ancient, city-spanning network of sealed conduits carrying steady heat. No civic authority claims authorship. No credible record shows installation. The system predates Round 0 AS.

### 18.1 Names & Common Usage
- **Official:** **Thermal Mains** (Conclave term), abbreviated **Mains**.  
- **Colloquial:** **Heat-lines**, **Deep Runs**, **Redmains**.  
- **Street:** “the warmbones.”

### 18.2 Physical Description (observed)
- Material: dark, matte alloy; welds unseen; rings at regular **Span** intervals.  
- Diameters: trunk mains ~1 chain; feeders ~½ chain; laterals ~¼ chain.  
- Heat: constant hand-warm; rises to hot during seasonal surges; never cold.  
- Sound: faint ticking at Bell changes in some districts.  
- Access: maintenance collars appear at junctions, but fasteners do not match known thread patterns.

### 18.3 Layout
- Every capital has at least one **trunk** loop circling core districts, with **feeders** to kilns, clinics, public baths, greenhouses, and winter shelters.  
- **Bridge district bypasses** exist in Underface cities to reduce thermal strain on spans.  
- **Pane quarter beds** in Crown cities use mains to stabilize anneal courtyards.

### 18.4 Operation (public practice)
- **Valves:** stamped plates with simple open/close levers; color code by city.  
- **Heat taps:** legal taps exist at civic sites (kilns, clinics); private taps require Conclave permit.  
- **Pressure:** no known pumps; flow appears self-equalizing.  
- **Outages:** rare, brief; systems re-warm without human intervention.

### 18.5 Governance & Law
- **Conclave custody** of maps and junction keys; cities claim easement rights.  
- **Mains Law:**  
  - No cutting, boring, or unlicensed tapping.  
  - Violations = seizure of shop and tools; Oath court referral.  
  - Conclave blue flag must fly at any mains access work.

### 18.6 Maintenance Protocols (what’s allowed)
- **Inspection only:** temperature checks, valve tests, leak spotting.  
- **Surface brace:** adding cradle blocks under exposed lengths.  
- **Collateral repair:** stonework around mains; do not stress the pipe.  
- **Forbidden:** grinding, welding, clamping that bites metal, Unline within 1 chain.

### 18.7 Hazards & Signs
- **Weeping heat:** fine steam through mortar; mark with yellow chevron and notify Conclave.  
- **Red-ring:** alloy band blushing red around a seam ring; clear public and post rolling bell.  
- **Rattle:** periodic clatter at Bell change; indicates feeder mis-set.  
- **Violet haze:** extremely rare; evacuate and call Hazard Crews.

### 18.8 Theories (public, unproven)
- **Divine Works:** built in the God War to stabilize Veins.  
- **Pre-Severing Artifice:** relic of a lost engineer civilization.  
- **Bore Feed:** the world’s “heart heat” is channeled along natural lines.  
- **Quietist Claim:** the Mains are the world’s cooling veins, proof that motion should cease.  
The Conclave prints **no** official position.

### 18.9 Integration by City (examples)
- **Grindheim:** mains feed bathhouses, winter kitchens, infirmaries; trunk loop skirts foundries to avoid over-threft.  
- **Cinderfell:** mains warm hazard depots; emergency shelters tied to red-main ports.  
- **Hammerpost:** mains run along canyon lip; bridge wardens keep bypass valves half-closed.  
- **Strathmoor:** mains stabilize anneal courts; White Kilns monitor purity drift.  
- **Aurelion:** mains heat oath halls in cold Turns; lens farms avoid mains to protect sand.  
- **Veslara:** clinic wards and wind-organ humidifiers tied to feeders.

### 18.10 Mapping & Symbols
- On plans, draw trunk mains as **thick red lines** with hollow circles every **Span**; feeders as thin red with solid dots. Junction numbers use city prefix (e.g., **GH-R12**).

### 18.11 Field Kit Add-Ons (for mains work)
- **Thermo-slips:** wax strips that darken at preset heats; tag to pipe for bell.  
- **Non-bite clamps:** wood saddles to brace without metal contact.  
- **Listening cone:** amplifies internal tick; used at rings.  
- **Valve keys:** city-coded; Conclave holds master blanks.

### 18.12 Incident Playbook (excerpt)
1) **Spot:** mark zone (yellow chevron).  
2) **Clear:** two Fields in all directions.  
3) **Notify:** Conclave + Hazard Crews (rolling bell).  
4) **Test:** thermo-slips at three points; record.  
5) **Set:** valves as instructed; never force.  
6) **Log:** witness marks; blue seal; reopen only on Assessor order.

### 18.13 Cultural Notes
- **Underface:** mains are “the warmbones;” taverns open their heat taps on festival nights.  
- **Crown:** mains keep clinics steady; households rent feeders in cold Turns.  
- **Seam Markets:** no private taps; violation ends stall license.

---

- §20 Bore-Rim Operating Rules (Cinderfell codex, violet-light protocols)  
- §21 Civic Iconography Pack (banners, seals, shop marks)

Use these sections to ground scenes, conflicts, and logistics across Novellas 1–3 without unlocking later-series knowledge.

# Create the Te'Oga Story Bible as a Markdown file
content = r"""# TE’Oga STORY BIBLE • MASTER REFERENCE (Innocent Edition)
**Version 1.2** • For internal use by authors/editors  
Scope: canonical facts only. Neutral manual voice. No meta, no hidden twists, no foreshadowing.

---

## 0) Quick Lexicon (terms you’ll actually use)
- **Vein**: inherent line of strength/continuity inside any thing (stone, metal, body, oath, city).
- **Threft**: pressure building inside a Vein when acted on by will, heat, weight or intent.
- **Harrow**: backlash when threft exceeds what the Vein can bear (snaps, collapses, riots, panics).
- **Brast / brasting** *(Tempered usage)*: to set a Vein true by force and correction.
- **Veld / velding** *(Breathwright usage)*: to guide a Vein true by flow and shaping.
- **Bridge**: to share load between Veins (people or objects).
- **Unline**: to deliberately break a Vein’s route to reset or sabotage.
- **Fray**: damage to one’s personal Vein from overuse (etched skin, tremor, memory “scuff”).
- **Hollowing**: severe post-work emptiness after deep brast/veld; judgment impaired.
- **Quieting**: a Vein goes still (peaceful stasis or final failure).
- **Cleavepoint**: exact instant two Veins meet and either bridge or break.

> **Usage note.** Use **brast** in Underface POV scenes and **veld** in Outer Crown POV scenes. “Veinwright” is neutral for all practitioners.

---

## 1) Cosmology & History (public canon)
### 1.1 The World
- **Name:** **Te’Oga**
- **Nature:** a wounded sphere. Centuries ago a white-core impact pierced one hemisphere without total destruction, leaving a planet-spanning tunnel.
- **The Bore:** the glowing wound; lumen (radiant crystal-fire) at depth. Weather, magnetism and gravity skew around it.

### 1.2 The Four Gods (the only gods)
Worshiped as real beings who once contended openly. Modern doctrine: they withdrew after the God War.
- **Xilcore** (Form). Sigil: hex. Virtue: structure, geometry, craft law. Vice: rigidity.
- **Blemo** (Purity). Sigil: clear drop. Virtue: refinement, cleansing, oaths. Vice: zeal.
- **Leesa** (Wholeness). Sigil: ring. Virtue: healing, joining, mercy. Vice: smothering unity.
- **Seeri** (Decay). Sigil: thorn spiral. Virtue: compost, renewal, endings as truth. Vice: rot for its own sake.

> **Counter-Spark** is a debated natural principle among Quietists, not a deity in orthodox canon.

### 1.3 The Severing (God War)
- Duration: several centuries of conflict culminating in the **Severing**.
- Event: the terminal strike burned a tunnel through the Underface hemisphere (the Bore), fractured plates, warped fields.
- Aftermath: the gods ceased manifest action.
- **Dating:** current year **Round 612 After Severing (612 AS)**.

### 1.4 Veins (accepted theory)
Scholars and priest-craft agree: all matter and society have **Veins** carrying **threft**. Skilled people can brast/veld Veins to avert **harrow**. Deepest Veins (the **Undercurrent**) are strongest near the Bore.

---

## 2) Geography & Capitals
### 2.1 Hemispheric Order
- **The Underface** (Tempered Realms; “Forge half”): semi-dark lands sloping toward the Bore’s red glow.
- **The Outer Crown** (Breathwright Domains; “Glass half”): bright lands refracting sun via high-air panes; frequent lightfall and mirage storms.
- **The Curtain:** ring of violent updrafts and storm shear girdling the transitional latitudes.
- **Riftlands:** shattered terrain between capitals with chasms and glassy scars.

**Distance Units**
- **Chain** = 20 paces  
- **Span** = 10 chains  
- **Field** = 10 spans (≈ a large sport-ground)  
Rift widths are measured in **Fields**.

### 2.2 The Six Capitals (each governs 3–4 satellites)
**Underface (Tempered Realms)**
1. **Grindheim** — basalt terraces, river-forges; guild seat of the Ember Crown.  
   Satellites: Redwater, Blackgate, Slagline.
2. **Cinderfell** — crater city near Bore rim; alloy markets, hazard crews.  
   Satellites: Ashmere, Vent’s End, Brim.
3. **Hammerpost** — canyon bridges, foundry schools; military logistics.  
   Satellites: Irontide, Rivet, Quarry Holt.

**Outer Crown (Breathwright Domains)**
4. **Strathmoor** — tiered glass domes; Collegium of Flows.  
   Satellites: Clearfen, Lumenport, Sandreach.
5. **Aurelion** — sun-lens farms; oath registries; perfume-salt trade.  
   Satellites: Brightfold, Prism Vale, Salt Step.
6. **Veslara** — cliff arches, wind organs; medica of Leesa.  
   Satellites: Glisten, Threadbay, Whisper Ridge.

**Routes**
- **Seam Roads** (dangerous cross-Curtain caravans)
- **Skylines** (tethered glider lanes in the Crown)
- **Gorgeways** (chain bridges and ferries in the Underface)

---

## 3) Society & Guild Systems
### 3.1 Neutral Guild Families
- **Veinwrights’ Conclave** (standards, audits)
- **Surveyors’ Chain** (maps, rift-measure, bridge charters)
- **Mercers’ Table** (weights, coin assay)
- **Couriers’ Bell** (sealed dispatch across Curtain)
- **Healers of the Line** (Leesa-leaning Vein care)

### 3.2 Principle-Aligned Guilds (respectable)
- **Xilcore:** Stonebinders; Alloy Collegium; Rulewrights (geometry law)
- **Blemo:** Purifiers (water, grain, rites); Oathkeepers (contract courts); White Kilns (fine glass)
- **Leesa:** Joiners (masonry unions); Roundhouse Medica (public hospitals); Breadwardens (stores)
- **Seeri:** Salvagers’ Ordinate (reuse); Final Measures (funerary); Rotgardeners (soil renewal)

### 3.3 Fringe & Heresies (gray/violent)
- **Quietists** (philosophy of stillness; “Quieting” as virtue)
- **Shatterhands** (political vandals; Unline to “reset”)
- **Bright Zeal** (Blemo extremists; “burn the taint”)
- **Form Absolutists** (Xilcore extremity; geometry over people)
- **Open Rot** (nihilist Seeri splinter; forced decay)

---

## 4) Religion & Public Rites
### 4.1 Temples & Sigils
- **Xilcore Houses** (hex flags; tool consecrations)
- **Blemo Baths** (ablutions; oath ledgers)
- **Leesa Rounds** (circles for vows and reconciliation)
- **Seeri Cloisters** (mortuaries; compost terraces)

### 4.2 Festivals (shared calendar)
- **Forge-Round** (Underface new year; first iron pour)
- **Clearwake** (Crown spring; first flawless pane)
- **Joining Day** (city amnesties and reconciliations)
- **Return to Soil** (autumn; Seeri thanksgiving)

### 4.3 The Quietist Debate
- **Claim:** the universe trends toward **Quieting**; the gods’ quarrel delays peace.
- **Orthodox response:** Quiet is symptom, not virtue; motion and form sustain life.
- **Status:** tolerated in the Crown; watched in the Underface.

---

## 5) Craft/Magic Mechanics (Vein Theory)
### 5.1 Capabilities
- **Brasting** (force-correction): set load-paths, steady spans/crowds, reinforce oaths.
- **Velding** (flow-guidance): control cool rates, divert heat “like breath,” ease negotiations, knit fractures.
- **Bridging:** pair Veins (teams, couples, beams) to share threft.
- **Measured Unline:** controlled break to reset bad lines (licensed).

### 5.2 Costs & Risks
- **Fray marks:** fine pale striations; tremor; breath-short; ear ring.
- **Hollowing:** erratic judgment; overwork temptations; touch-seeking.
- **Harrow events:** stampedes, bridge failures; always track **load, heat, will**.

### 5.3 Training Track
- **Apprentice → Journeyman → Warden → Master → Assessor** (see §17). Dual-trained exist, but licensure is typically single-path to prevent abuse.

### 5.4 Taboos
- **Blood-brast** (forcing a living Vein under oath)
- **Glass-gelding** (permanent Unline removing a person’s capacity to bridge)
- **Unlicensed mass-work** during festivals

---

## 6) Economy & Technology (Renaissance tipping to proto-industrial)
- **Power:** bellows, water wheels, wind towers, lens furnaces.
- **Transport:** chain-bridges, ore-sleds, sand-skiffs, tethered gliders (Crown).
- **Black powder:** quarry/signal/ceremonial only.
- **Printing:** woodblock + early movable type; contract ledgers common.
- **Combustion studies:** **Fire-engines** (sealed pistons) in Cinderfell labs; experimental; not field-deployed in Series 1–2.
- **Currency:** **Marks** (iron), **Crowns** (silver), **Lumen** chits (rare, near Bore).
- **Trade:** ore/tools (Underface) ↔ glass/salts/medica (Crown).

---

## 7) Civic Systems
### 7.1 Law
- **Capitals Compact:** six capitals pledge road safety, fair weights, extradition of saboteurs.
- **Guild Courts:** craft disputes; temple courts handle oaths.
- **Vein Statutes:** all mass-work logged; wardens may halt unsafe builds.

### 7.2 Measures
- **Distance:** Chain / Span / Field
- **Weight:** Stone / Bar / Load
- **Heat:** **Bell** count (forge); **Shade Steps** (0–12) for glass
- **Time:** Day = **Bell-cycle** (eight named bells) • Week = **Hand** (5 days) • Month = **Turn** (6 Hands) • Year = **Round** (12 Turns) • Current: **Round 612 AS**

### 7.3 Calendar Highlights
- **Borewatch** (equinox): both hemispheres mark the same twilight surge.
- **Oath-Renewal** (mid-year): Blemo rites; contracts re-inked.

---

## 8) Political Blocks
- **Tempered Triad** (Grindheim–Cinderfell–Hammerpost): pro-Xilcore law, controlled Seeri salvage, skeptical of Quietism.
- **Crown Collegia** (Strathmoor–Aurelion–Veslara): Leesa public health, Blemo oaths, tolerant of debate.
- **Seam Markets** (neutral): black/gray hubs at Curtain crossings; Conclave jurisdiction.

---

## 9) Character Dossiers (public, in-world)
> Ages and specifics are guide rails; adjust for scene needs but keep arcs consistent.

### 9.1 Jhace of Grindheim
- **Origin:** Grindheim, Underface
- **Craft:** Veinwright (brast-dominant), bridgewright schooling
- **Traits:** disciplined, plain-spoken, instinct for cleavepoints
- **Faith:** Xilcore customary rites; respects Seeri pragmatics
- **Goal (public):** keep spans standing; keep crews alive
- **Flaw (observed):** over-correction; takes others’ load until fray
- **Ties:** Luthen (former master), Conclave assessors

### 9.2 Tiffani of Strathmoor
- **Origin:** Strathmoor, Outer Crown
- **Craft:** Veinwright (veld-dominant), glass medica research
- **Traits:** incisive, patient, improvisational; reads “bubbles” in systems
- **Faith:** Leesa rites; ethical Blemo oaths
- **Goal (public):** codify safe cool-rates for civic glass and public health
- **Flaw (observed):** rejects “good enough” when a purer solution exists
- **Ties:** Collegium of Flows, Roundhouse Medica

### 9.3 Brighlee (Aurelion)
- **Role:** lens-smith’s apprentice turned courier (Couriers’ Bell)
- **Craft:** veld-adjacent; exceptional at bridging group intent under pressure
- **Traits:** upbeat, logistical genius, refuses to panic
- **Arc:** becomes movement lead across the Curtain

### 9.4 Sha’niah (Cinderfell)
- **Role:** hazard-crew warden; Bore-rim responder
- **Craft:** brast professional; reads heat by breath and foot feel
- **Traits:** blunt, steady, quietly protective
- **Arc:** anchors Underface operations; teaches safe brast under shock

### 9.5 Valari (Veslara)
- **Role:** medica-joiner; Leesa-trained healer specializing in Vein care
- **Craft:** veld healer; pioneers fray-mark treatments
- **Traits:** calm, exacting, unafraid to deny unsafe work
- **Arc:** moral center; enforces rest; rejects blood-brast

### 9.6 Chloen (Grindheim)
- **Role:** Surveyors’ Chain scribe/cartographer
- **Craft:** neutral; maps cleavepoints and rift dynamics
- **Traits:** observant, dry wit, loves ledgers
- **Arc:** proves maps prevent harrow as surely as hammers

### 9.7 Kylar (Strathmoor)
- **Role:** Oathkeepers’ militia escort; Aurelion Registry detachment
- **Craft:** oath discipline; tactical small-load brast for crowd control
- **Traits:** loyal, rules-first, quietly brave
- **Arc:** bends rigid oaths toward merciful outcomes

### 9.8 The Auditor (office)
- **Definition:** civil office within the Conclave; audits Vein logs, certifies mass-work, investigates harrow
- **Powers:** halt orders; seizure of unsafe sites; cross-border writs
- **Perception:** necessary but resented
- **Note:** an office with rotating officers, not a singular figure

---

## 10) Visual Symbols & Color Keys
- **Xilcore:** iron/black, hex marks
- **Blemo:** white/clear, drop sigils
- **Leesa:** green/gold, circles
- **Seeri:** brown/rust, thorn spiral
- **Quietists:** grey cloth bands; blank circle pin
- **Conclave:** blue stamp with crossed chain and quill

---

## 11) Safety Protocols (world logic to respect)
- Any mass-work in public must be logged with the **Veinwrights’ Conclave**.
- Festivals increase threft; no unlicensed brast/veld in crowds.
- Fray is cumulative; characters cannot “push through” indefinitely.
- Blood-brast is a crime in both hemispheres.
- Crossing the Curtain requires a **Seam Writ**; storms can erase maps weekly.

---

## 12) Political Case Studies (one per capital)

### 12.1 Grindheim
**Governance.** Guild-municipal council chaired by the **Ember Crown** Warden; standing seats for Conclave and Stonebinders.  
**Power blocs.** Ember Crown; Stonebinders; Salvagers’ Ordinate; Oathkeepers tribunal.  
**Disputes.** Furnace rations; bridge-rights over Blackgate Gorge; scrap claims.  
**Levers.** Furnace ledgers; rivergate tariffs; Conclave halt orders.  
**Cross-border.** Trades iron bar for Crown salts/medica; skeptical of Quietists.  
**Field guidance.** Wear Conclave blue when brasting; post a Bell-runner; never Unline a canal wall without writ.

### 12.2 Cinderfell
**Governance.** Tri-warden: Alloy Collegium, Hazard Crews, Surveyors’ Chain.  
**Power blocs.** Hazard Crews; Alloy labs; Couriers’ Bell (exclusive route).  
**Disputes.** Lab safety vs quotas; courier exclusivity; salvage rights after light-surges.  
**Levers.** Bore-rim access; hazard stipends; drill closures.  
**Cross-border.** Pragmatic trade in alloy recipes for salts and sand.  
**Field guidance.** Heat-shade cloaks (Steps 8–12); log all work; if light turns violet, clear to second lines.

### 12.3 Hammerpost
**Governance.** Marshal-Magistrate; canyon logistics hub.  
**Power blocs.** Bridgewright Corps; Rivet Quarter Syndics; Final Measures.  
**Disputes.** Tolls; weight limits; funerary route priority post-collapse.  
**Levers.** Toll exemptions; Gorgeway access; burial allocations.  
**Cross-border.** Suspicious of “airy” Crown goods.  
**Field guidance.** Stamped load sheets; tool inspection; demo brast required for foreign wardens.

### 12.4 Strathmoor
**Governance.** Collegium Council; First Provost by seniority.  
**Power blocs.** Collegium of Flows; White Kilns; Roundhouse Medica.  
**Disputes.** Research secrecy vs safety; pane export quotas; medica formula access.  
**Levers.** Kiln schedules; publication licenses; apprentice lists.  
**Cross-border.** Trades lenses/medical ware for alloy; tolerates Quietists if clinics undisturbed.  
**Field guidance.** Announce veld intent; no sand-throwing without sandmaster; apprentices observe.

### 12.5 Aurelion
**Governance.** Civic Senate; Oath Ledgers central.  
**Power blocs.** Oathkeepers’ Registry; Lens-Farm Syndics; Mercers’ Table.  
**Disputes.** False-oath cases; light-rights; coin shortages during storm-season.  
**Levers.** Oath festivals; lens angle decrees; embargoes.  
**Cross-border.** Active diplomacy with Grindheim; largest Seam Market.  
**Field guidance.** Ledgers straight; random bond checks; have an Oathkeeper announce small-load veld for crowd calm.

### 12.6 Veslara
**Governance.** Elder Council; Leesa medica and Joiners dominant.  
**Power blocs.** Roundhouse Medica; Joiners’ Circle; Wind-Organ Conservators.  
**Disputes.** Clinic triage in plagues; union protections vs emergency works; organ preservation vs expansion.  
**Levers.** Clinic access; union endorsements; rite schedules.  
**Cross-border.** Bridges to Hammerpost; medica↔stone expertise exchange.  
**Field guidance.** Enforce Warden rest; never interrupt organ rites (also a crowd-safety signal).

---

## 13) Field Kits & Tool Glossaries

### 13.1 Veinwright (Brast) Kit — Underface
Hammers (face/cross-peen/rounding, 1–3); wedges/dogs; tongs; feeler irons; heat bell cards; quench vessels (oil/water/brine); line chalk (iron-red); ledger & Conclave seal; leather hood; bracers; bell whistle; optional Xilcore tool-string.

### 13.2 Veinwright (Veld) Kit — Crown
Blowpipes (short/long); punty rods; marvers/paddles; blocks/jacks; anneal sand (Shade Steps 3–9) & cradles; shade gauge; thread-clamps; line ink (pale gold); sleeve guards; breath-veil; water ladle; burn salve; kiln ledger; White Kiln stamps.

### 13.3 Surveyors’ Chain Kit
Chains (tagged); plumb lines/levels; sight boards; rift probes; map folio; signal flags; bell-code card.

### 13.4 Couriers’ Bell Kit
Runner satchel; bell pair; writ wallet (Seam, temple, guild); ridge-grip shoes; reversible cloak; rations/salts; rope knife; Conclave pin.

### 13.5 Hazard Crew Kit (Cinderfell)
Heat cloaks (Shade 10–12); hook poles; water lines; breath buckets; stretcher/splints; ward ropes; siren cones.

### 13.6 Medica Vein-Care Kit (Veslara)
Fray salves; cooling stones; pulse bands; needles/thread; clinic tokens; portable quiet screens.

### 13.7 Auditor Inspection Pack
Writ case; measuring rod; inks/seals; witness ledger; badge; witness pins.

### 13.8 Oathkeeper Militia Kit (Aurelion)
Short batons; bond cords; shield boards; horn pair; writ folders.

### 13.9 Seam Market Trader Kit
Neutral stall cloths; dual weight sets; indicator cards; two-key lockbox; weekly safe-exit map.

### 13.10 Bore-Rim Expedition Kit (restricted)
Goggles (violet filter); anchors/lines (glass-bite tips); heat drums; violet tracer chalk; recall flag.

### 13.11 Tool Glossary (selected)
Cleave-iron; bridge-pin; shade hood; bell cards; line ink; fray band.
