# §24 • Foodways & Material Culture (Glasspunk/Forgepunk Baseline)

> Practical reference for daily life, props, and believable background action. Keep materials local and hand-wrought. Avoid anachronistic conveniences.

### 24.1 Fuels & Heat Sources
- **Thermal Mains:** civic baseline heat (see §18). Used for baths, clinics, anneal courts, winter kitchens.  
- **Charcoal & Coke:** Underface forges; graded by Bell heat cards.  
- **Lens Furnaces:** Crown heat via sun-lens arrays; weather dependent.  
- **Timber & Reed Bricks:** rural satellites; low ash, slow heat.  
- **Oil & Tallow:** lamps, shop heat at night; ash-boar fat common in Underface.

### 24.2 Kitchens & Food Prep
- **Underface staples:** barley, iron-pot stews, salt-preserved meats, root mash.  
- **Crown staples:** flatbreads, glass-kettle teas, citrus-salted greens, chilled sweets.  
- **Seam Market fare:** skewers, pickled fruits, caravan mix.  
- **Cooking tech:**  
  - **Griddle stones** on Thermal taps.  
  - **Hanging kettles** over charcoal cages (portable).  
  - **Sand ovens** in Crown (retained heat; good for slow breads).  
- **Preservation:** brine casks, vinegar jars, pane sugar candies, smokehouses.

### 24.3 Drink
- **Water standards:** Blemo Baths test and stamp cisterns; street fountains at Conclave-marked nodes.  
- **Common drinks:** small beers, reed tea, citrus water, kiln-porter (dark, malty).  
- **Medicinals:** willow bark infusion (fray pain), salt tabs (Curtain crossings).

### 24.4 Clothing & Workwear
- **Underface cut:** layered wool and leather; tool loops; forearm bracers; bell-whistle lanyards.  
- **Crown cut:** light linens, sleeve guards, breath-veils; shade hoods for veld.  
- **Badges & marks:** guild pins on right breast; Conclave blue ribbon for site authority; temple tokens on belts.  
- **Weather gear:** curtain-cloaks (wind-lashed seams), dust veils (Hammerpost), glare goggles (Strathmoor).

### 24.5 Housing & Shops
- **Underface:** stone terraces, iron lintels, cellar heat cupboards off mains; drain channels flagged in red.  
- **Crown:** pane roofs with shade-louver frames; cooling courts; reed mats; wind-catcher vents.  
- **Workshops:**  
  - **Forge bays**: bellows pit, quench trench, tool board, ledger niche.  
  - **Glass houses**: anneal racks on wheels, sand bins by grade, shade-lanterns.  
  - **Clinics**: Quiet rooms, fray band cupboard, wash cistern, green chits drawer.

### 24.6 Street Hardware & Furniture
- Stone benches anchored near spans, water jars at every second corner, bell posts at junctions, shade screens strung across Crown alleys.

### 24.7 Tools in Daily Life (non-guild)
- **Home kits:** patch needles, cord braids, charcoal pinchers, jar seals.  
- **Market kits:** weight stones, slate tallies, stall cloths, lockboxes.  
- **Travel kits:** ridge shoes, cloak pins, salt tabs, rope knives, Seam Writ sleeves.

### 24.8 Festivals & Food
- **Forge-Round:** first pour stew, coin-cakes on new spans, hammer-chime parades.  
- **Clearwake:** bubble breads, iced fruits, pane lanterns hung across streets.  
- **Joining Day:** shared loaves, oath broths at temple courts.

### 24.9 Waste, Salvage, Repair
- **Seeri Cloisters** collect compost and scrap; Rotgardeners run terrace beds.  
- **Repair culture:** patch before replace; “three mendings” rule for clothes; tools re-hafted, re-ground, recorded in shop ledgers.

---

# §25 • Language & Naming (Orthography, Forms, Profanity Map)

> Keep names pronounceable, grounded, and region-coded. Language is a tool: it reveals class, guild, and city.

### 25.1 Orthography & Sound Tendencies
- **Underface speech:** hard stops, short vowels; clipped forms.  
- **Crown speech:** open vowels, soft fricatives; formal diction in public.  
- **Transliteration marks:**  
  - Apostrophes mark elision or glottal stop (e.g., **Sha’niah**).  
  - Accents rare; avoid clusters that stall speech.  
  - Double consonants for weight in Underface (e.g., **Cinderfell**).

### 25.2 Personal Names
- **Given names** followed by **of [City]** in formal contexts: *Jhace of Grindheim*.  
- **Guild sobriquets** replace family names in some Underface contracts: *Jhace, Bridgewright*.  
- **Crown convention:** full name plus House affiliation in ledgers: *Tiffani, Collegium of Flows*.  
- **Children:** diminutives common; apprentices adopt “-hand” or “-mind” honorifics in public.

### 25.3 Surnames & By-names
- **Underface:** trade-based or site-based by-names (Blackgate, Slagline).  
- **Crown:** lineages kept; also pane- or kiln-based by-names (White Kiln, Clearfen).  
- **Seam Markets:** ledger names for neutrality; true names optional.

### 25.4 Toponyms (place naming)
- **Underface:** material + feature (Iron-tide, Hammer-post).  
- **Crown:** clarity + terrain (Clear-fen, Prism Vale).  
- **Riftlands:** hazards as names (Break’s Edge, Fall Field).

### 25.5 Address & Titles
- **Craft:** wright, warden, assessor, master, apprentice.  
- **Civic:** provost, marshal, elder.  
- **Religious:** keeper (Blemo), binder (Xilcore), round-mother/father (Leesa), reader-of-ends (Seeri).  
- **Polite forms:** Goodhand, Goodmind.

### 25.6 Oaths & Formal Phrases
- **Blemo oath entry:** “Ink laid, bond straight.”  
- **Xilcore tool blessing:** “Edge true, face steady.”  
- **Leesa reconciliation:** “Circle holds.”  
- **Seeri closing:** “Back to soil.”

### 25.7 Proverbs & Working Idiom
- “Three bells to cool, two to speak, one to strike.”  
- “Mark the line or mark the grave.”  
- “Mind your shade.”  
- “Lay a bridge.”

### 25.8 Profanity & Vulgarity (safe list)
- **Underface:** “slag,” “all bell, no heat,” “mains-sucker,” “span-coward.”  
- **Crown:** “too clear by half,” “bubble-brain,” “shade-blind,” “oath-stained.”  
- **Universal soft curses:** “by the line,” “thorn take it.”  
- Avoid anachronistic modern swears; keep insults tied to craft failure, cowardice, false oaths, or wasted material.

### 25.9 Writing Conventions (for the text)
- Use **brast** vs **veld** by POV region.  
- Keep guild names uppercase on first mention, lowercase afterward if generic.  
- Do not italicize common in-world terms (Vein, threft, Harrow) unless defining.

---

# §26 • Education & Apprenticeship Systems (Licensure & Seals)

> How people become competent without magic handwaving. Use these rails to make competence feel earned.

### 26.1 Childhood & Basic Schooling
- **Underface:** community halls teach counting (load math), tool names, bell codes, ledger basics by **Round 8–10**.  
- **Crown:** House schools teach letters, shade steps, hand signals, basic Pane lore by **Round 8–10**.  
- **Shared:** temple catechisms; safety mnemonics; civic courtesy.

### 26.2 Choosing a Path
- **Underface:** parents petition a guild; **Trial Hand** (5 days) shadowing a shop.  
- **Crown:** **Trial Week** at Houses; scores posted at dusk; apprentices chosen on merit and oaths.  
- **Seam:** Conclave notice boards list open apprenticeships across the Curtain.

### 26.3 Apprenticeship (years and rails)
- **Length:** typically **6–12 Turns** to Journeyman.  
- **Curriculum:**  
  - **Brast path:** hammer control, load math, pier setting, controlled unline, crowd drills.  
  - **Veld path:** shade gauge reading, blowpipe work, anneal schedules, sand grades, cooling rescue.  
  - **Shared:** ledger discipline, Conclave statutes, first aid, festival protocols.  
- **Assessment:** quarterly shop tests; annual Conclave inspection.

### 26.4 Exams & Licensure
- **Journeyman Exam:** supervised job from plan to closeout; witness ledger signed; Conclave oral on law.  
- **Warden Exam:** crew management on a live site; halt-order mock; emergency drill.  
- **Master Review:** body of work, letters from two Assessors, innovations or training record.  
- **Assessor Appointment:** by Conclave council; oath to neutrality; rotating jurisdictions.

### 26.5 Seals & Papers
- **Apprentice token:** stamped copper, shop sigil.  
- **Journeyman license:** parchment with Conclave blue seal; tool list entered.  
- **Warden stamp:** personal blue seal ring; authority to post blue flags.  
- **Assessor badge:** enamel blue, chain+quill.

### 26.6 Cross-Training & Dual Paths
- Allowed with sponsor letters from both a Forge shop and a Glass House.  
- **Minimum gaps:** 2 Turns between Journeyman licenses to reduce fray and Hollowing.  
- **Restrictions:** no solo mass-work when switching crafts for first 3 Hands.

### 26.7 Discipline & Expulsion
- **Minor breaches:** written admonish; fines; supervised re-training.  
- **Major breaches:** suspension (Hands/Turns), tool seizure, public posting.  
- **Grave breaches:** blood-brast, mass fraud, mains tampering → expulsion, Oath court referral.

### 26.8 Continuing Practice
- **Clinics & Rest:** mandated rest cycles after Harrow; fray band checks logged.  
- **Refresher Bells:** quarterly drills on crowd control, bell codes, shade signals.  
- **Innovation writs:** propose new tools or methods; Conclave trial yard tests.

### 26.9 Public Lectures & Guilderies
- **Underface:** Bridgewright talks in tavern lofts, tool auctions, scrap lotteries.  
- **Crown:** Collegium demonstrations, pane exhibitions, purity debates with White Kilns.  
- **Seam:** mixed craft fairs; Conclave “safe practice” days.

# §27 • Security, Militias & Warfare (Civic Force, Not Empire Fantasy)

> Defense is municipal, guild-backed, and logistics-heavy. Wars are campaigns of bridges, lanes, clinics, and ledgers.

### 27.1 Force Types (by city)

- **Oathkeepers (Aurelion-led bloc)**  
  Civic militia. Contract-first, formation discipline, crowd spacing, arrests.  
  Core tools: batons, shield boards, horn pair, bond cords.  
  Specialty: mediation lines in markets and gateways.

- **Bridgewright Corps (Hammerpost)**  
  Combat engineers. Build/deny spans, rig demolitions, weight-control on routes.  
  Core tools: bridge-pins, chain winches, powder for abutments.  
  Specialty: choke-point control, rapid span throws across rifts.

- **Hazard Crews (Cinderfell)**  
  Search-and-rescue, firebreaks, heat-line incidents, riot fire control.  
  Core tools: heat cloaks, hooks, water lines, siren cones.  
  Specialty: violet retreat, public extraction, thermal main perimeters.

- **Conclave Wardens (all capitals)**  
  Site-security during mass-work; halt enforcement.  
  Core tools: writs, blue flags, witness ledgers.  
  Specialty: neutral authority between guilds and temples.

- **Temple Auxiliaries**  
  Leesa stretcher-bearers, Blemo oath scribes, Xilcore line-holders, Seeri mortuary teams.  
  Specialty: morale, order, handling dead and frightened.

### 27.2 Doctrine

- **Underface:** hold lines; protect spans; weight vs shock.  
- **Outer Crown:** disperse glare-panicked crowds; shade-step control; water and wind lanes.  
- **Seam Markets:** de-escalation; witness-first; trade resumes fast.

### 27.3 Formations (standard)

- **Rope-Line:** ten with waist cords for rifts.  
- **Shield Wedge:** Oathkeepers’ push to extract a target.  
- **Spiral Flow:** Crown crowd rotation to bleed pressure.  
- **Bridge Box:** Corps secures abutments, powder prepped but capped; Conclave logs.

### 27.4 Campaign Logistics

- **Food & water:** tavern contracts; cistern rights; salt tabs for the Curtain.  
- **Medical:** Roundhouse triage tents at every second field post.  
- **Signals:** horns (low halt/high move), bells (see §16.4), chimes (clinic routing).  
- **Ledgers:** supply tallies, arrest lists, casualty sheets, writs.

### 27.5 Siege & Denial (rare, brief)

- **Denial tools:** chain cuts, span drops, lens covers, cistern seizure.  
- **Rules:** clinics and bath houses neutral; Thermal Mains off-limits.  
- **Powder:** structural only; no anti-personnel shrapnel.

### 27.6 Typical Threats

- **Shatterhands:** unline saboteurs.  
- **Bright Zeal:** purity pogroms, book burnings, clinic harassment.  
- **Bandits:** toll fraud, market theft, caravan hits.  
- **Quietist uprushes:** mass sit-stills blocking logistics.

### 27.7 Arrest to Adjudication

- **Arrest:** two witnesses, horn call, bond cord logged.  
- **Holding:** city cells; food and clinic access.  
- **Temple/Oath court:** quick hearings; fines, labor, or exile by Hand/Turn.

---

# §28 • Crime, Black Markets & Law in Practice

> Nothing breaks immersion like spotless cities. Use this as your grime layer.

### 28.1 Common Offenses

- **Weights & Measures:** shaved stones, false scales.  
- **Seal Work:** forged Conclave stamps, counterfeit Oath slips.  
- **Mains Taps:** illegal heat spurs to private shops.  
- **Shop Crimes:** tool theft, ledger tamper, apprentice poaching.  
- **Festival Breaches:** unlicensed mass-work, crowd incitement.  
- **Vein Crimes:** blood-brast, forced unline, clinic denial.

### 28.2 Black Market Circuits

- **Underface:** slag alleys behind foundries; scrap lots; night tolls on minor spans.  
- **Outer Crown:** pane-back rooms; sand grade swaps; lens angle rents.  
- **Seam:** moving auctions after bell, Mercers’ eyes elsewhere.

### 28.3 Smuggling Routes

- **Rift skirts:** rope ladders, goat trains.  
- **Curtain slips:** glider drops during soft shear.  
- **Bath tunnels:** Blemo drains repurposed; risky, often fatal.

### 28.4 Scams & Cons

- **Blue Ribbon Borrow:** fake Conclave ribbons to shoo crowds.  
- **Heat Weep Panic:** staged steam near pipes to loot markets.  
- **Oath Swap:** switch bond pages; keep countersign.  
- **Shade Shuffle:** swap higher-grade sand sacks with wet fill.

### 28.5 Sentencing Norms

- **Fines:** Marks/Crowns scaled by risk and harm.  
- **Labor:** bridge repair, kiln cleaning, clinic latrines.  
- **Exile:** by Hands/Turns beyond city bounds.  
- **Temple Penitence:** ritual work and public apologies.  
- **Capital:** only for blood-brast and mass murder; rare; temple-led.

### 28.6 How Law Actually Works (write it this way)

- Paper trails matter more than speeches.  
- Witness Pair culture keeps officials honest.  
- Clinics and bath houses are untouchable; breaking this turns crowds against you.  
- Conclave blue is obeyed, but grumbled at. Respect the grumble.

---

# §29 • Trade & Macroeconomy (How Cities Breathe)

> If it can’t move, it dies. If it can’t be weighed, it’s a rumor.

### 29.1 Money

- **Marks (iron):** day labor, food, tools.  
- **Crowns (silver):** contracts, fines, rents, apprenticeships.  
- **Lumen chits:** rare Bore-rim notes for hazard trades.  
- **Temple tokens:** clinic priority (Leesa), bath access (Blemo).

### 29.2 Markets

- **Daily:** ward markets by craft, dawn to Seventh bell.  
- **Seam markets:** neutral days posted; storm clauses cancel.  
- **Fairs:** Turn-end craft fairs; exam showcases; Conclave safety days.

### 29.3 Flows (core exchanges)

- **Underface exports:** ore, forged tools, span service.  
- **Crown exports:** lenses, medical glass, salts, refined water.  
- **Seam services:** arbitration, weights, courier staging.

### 29.4 Tariffs & Ledgers

- **Route books:** tolls by chain, weight brackets, storm surcharges.  
- **Temple dues:** optional, buys goodwill and fast rites.  
- **Conclave fees:** audit, writ, and seal schedules.

### 29.5 Shocks & Buffers

- **Curtain storms:** routes close; food prices spike; temple kitchens open.  
- **Bore surges:** alloy scarcity; clinic demand; hazard pay rises.  
- **Dust years:** glass yield drops; pane repair delayed; crowd heat rises.  
- **Buffers:** grain stores, salt stocks, tool stocks; public posting by city.

### 29.6 Labor & Wages (ballpark)

- **Apprentice:** board + small Marks; festival bonus.  
- **Journeyman:** steady Marks; tool allowance; clinic chits.  
- **Warden:** higher Marks + blue seal stipend.  
- **Assessor/Provost:** salary; oath bond; no side work allowed.

---

# §30 • Technology Roadmap & Constraints (Stay Glasspunk/Forgepunk)

> Guardrails so the world doesn’t slide into gadgets. Everything is hand-wrought, lens-boosted, or mass-worked. No clockwork robots, no instant engines.

### 30.1 Present Tech (Series 1–2)

- **Heat:** Thermal Mains, charcoal/coke, sun-lens furnaces.  
- **Power:** water wheels, wind towers, hand treadles.  
- **Mechanics:** pulleys, gear trains, screw jacks, chain hoists.  
- **Materials:** iron/steel by bell cards; blown/annealed glass by shade steps.  
- **Comms:** bells, horns, hand signs, couriers, placards.  
- **Print:** woodblock + early moveable type; posted ledgers.

### 30.2 Emerging Tech (Series 3–4, controlled)

- **Fire-engines:** sealed piston experiments; stationary pumps in Cinderfell; no vehicles.  
- **Lens arrays:** larger civic furnaces; clinic sterilizing lamps.  
- **Span gauges:** portable deflection readers for bridges.  
- **Fray bands:** improved color-fast cloths calibrated to load/heat.

### 30.3 Prohibited or Distant

- Handheld firearms as standard kit; chemical cartridges; full steam locomotion; telegraph.  
- Anything that trivializes distance, crowd control, or heat.

### 30.4 Innovation Process

- **Writ:** propose to Conclave; post in trial yard; collect witness marks.  
- **Trial:** two Hands of field use under blue flag.  
- **Seal:** city adoption; maintenance schedule posted; failure class added to §19.

---

## Appendix A • Measurement Tables & Conversions

### A.1 Distance
- **1 Chain = 20 paces**  
- **1 Span = 10 Chains**  
- **1 Field = 10 Spans**

### A.2 Time
- **1 Day = Bell-cycle (8 named bells)**  
- **1 Hand = 5 Days**  
- **1 Turn = 6 Hands**  
- **1 Round = 12 Turns**

### A.3 Heat & Light Crosswalk
- **Shade Steps (glass):** 0 cool, 3 workable, 6 bright, 9 risky, 12 blinding.  
- **Forge Bells (metal):** 1 warm, 3 red, 5 orange, 7 yellow, 9 white.

### A.4 Weight
- **1 Load = 12 Stones**  
- **1 Bar = 3 Stones**  
- **1 Stone = 8 weight-pounds** (in-world standard, not Earth)

### A.5 Altitude Corrections (rule of thumb)
- For every **Span** above river level, reduce shade step by 1 for anneal timing; add one bell to cool for iron.

---

## Appendix B • Map & Icon Sheets (Writer’s Key)

- **Lines:** primary road (═), gorgeway (═|═), seam route (– – –△), heat main (██○○), glass main (▔▁▔▁).  
- **Sites:** Conclave house ■+quill; clinic ⭕; hazard ▲; auditor ⬣; market ▢; temple ◯ with god sigil.  
- **Flags:** red halt ■; blue work ■; yellow chevron ◀.  
- **Bell codes placard:** single long = clear span; two short = brace; three fast = Harrow; rolling = hazard right-of-way.  
- **Poster templates:** “Keep Lanes Clear,” “Mind Your Shade,” “Witness Needed.”

Checklist: ledger review • load test • bell count • fray check • reopen/deny

## Appendix D • Tool & Material Specs (Quick Tables)

### D.1 Alloys (Underface)
- **A-1 Span Steel:** bell 7 set, quench oil, temper 3; bridge pins, dogs.  
- **A-2 Tool Steel:** bell 8 set, quench brine, temper 2; hammers, chisels.  
- **A-3 Spring Iron:** bell 6 set, water cool; rope hooks, clamps.

### D.2 Glass Grades (Outer Crown)
- **G-1 Lens Clear:** shade 7–8, anneal 3 Hands; optics, clinics.  
- **G-2 Civic Pane:** shade 6–7, anneal 2 Hands; doors, roofs.  
- **G-3 Utility:** shade 5–6, anneal 1 Hand; jars, lanterns.

### D.3 Sand & Flux
- **Fine A:** high silica, low iron; add salt-soda in Clear Turns.  
- **Fine B:** balanced silica; standard flux.  
- **Coarse:** construction; add lime for stability.

### D.4 Fray Band Calibration
- **Pale shift at:** 2 Field hours heavy work or shade 7 for 1 Bell.  
- **Clinic threshold:** two pales in one Hand.

---

## Appendix E • Chapter Access Protocols (Framework Only)

> Use this to throttle what the drafting model can pull from the Bible per novella/series. No beats, just toggles.

### E.1 Locks (Always Locked Until Unlocked)
- **Bore-rim violet specifics:** locked until Novella 2 midpoint.  
- **Fire-engine details:** locked until Series 2.  
- **Temple politics depth:** locked until Series 2 mid.  
- **Black market routes:** partial exposure only in early novellas.

### E.2 Allowed From Page One
- §0–§7 base world rules; §11 safety; §13 kits; public rites; market logic; training paths.

### E.3 Progressive Unlocks (example rails)
- **Novella 1:** §12 (one city), §14 culture basics, §18 Thermal Mains as background utility.  
- **Novella 2:** add §20 Bore rules, §22 climate, §23 fauna/plants.  
- **Novella 3:** add §27 formations, §28 law-in-practice.  
- **Series 2:** partial §30 roadmap, fuller §29 macroeconomy, Appendix D advanced specs.

### E.4 Redlines (Never Allowed)
- No deus ex machina tech; no offscreen mass-work fixes; no blood-brast by protagonists; no Thermal Main sabotage by heroes.

# Build the complete Markdown file for download
content = r"""# TE’Oga STORY BIBLE • MASTER REFERENCE (Innocent Edition)
**Version 1.3** • For internal use by authors/editors  
Scope: canonical facts only. Neutral manual voice. No meta, no hidden twists, no foreshadowing.

---

## 0) Quick Lexicon (terms you’ll actually use)
- **Vein**: inherent line of strength/continuity inside any thing (stone, metal, body, oath, city).
- **Threft**: pressure building inside a Vein when acted on by will, heat, weight or intent.
- **Harrow**: backlash when threft exceeds what the Vein can bear (snaps, collapses, riots, panics).
- **Brast / brasting** *(Tempered usage)*: to set a Vein true by force and correction.
- **Veld / velding** *(Breathwright usage)*: to guide a Vein true by flow and shaping.
- **Bridge**: to share load between Veins (people or objects).
- **Unline**: to deliberately break a Vein’s route to reset or sabotage.
- **Fray**: damage to one’s personal Vein from overuse (etched skin, tremor, memory “scuff”).
- **Hollowing**: severe post-work emptiness after deep brast/veld; judgment impaired.
- **Quieting**: a Vein goes still (peaceful stasis or final failure).
- **Cleavepoint**: exact instant two Veins meet and either bridge or break.

> **Usage note.** Use **brast** in Underface POV scenes and **veld** in Outer Crown POV scenes. “Veinwright” is neutral for all practitioners.

---

## 1) Cosmology & History (public canon)
### 1.1 The World
- **Name:** **Te’Oga**
- **Nature:** a wounded sphere. Centuries ago a white-core impact pierced one hemisphere without total destruction, leaving a planet-spanning tunnel.
- **The Bore:** the glowing wound; lumen (radiant crystal-fire) at depth. Weather, magnetism and gravity skew around it.

### 1.2 The Four Gods (the only gods)
Worshiped as real beings who once contended openly. Modern doctrine: they withdrew after the God War.
- **Xilcore** (Form). Sigil: hex. Virtue: structure, geometry, craft law. Vice: rigidity.
- **Blemo** (Purity). Sigil: clear drop. Virtue: refinement, cleansing, oaths. Vice: zeal.
- **Leesa** (Wholeness). Sigil: ring. Virtue: healing, joining, mercy. Vice: smothering unity.
- **Seeri** (Decay). Sigil: thorn spiral. Virtue: compost, renewal, endings as truth. Vice: rot for its own sake.

> **Counter-Spark** is a debated natural principle among Quietists, not a deity in orthodox canon.

### 1.3 The Severing (God War)
- Duration: several centuries of conflict culminating in the **Severing**.
- Event: the terminal strike burned a tunnel through the Underface hemisphere (the Bore), fractured plates, warped fields.
- Aftermath: the gods ceased manifest action.
- **Dating:** current year **Round 612 After Severing (612 AS)**.

### 1.4 Veins (accepted theory)
Scholars and priest-craft agree: all matter and society have **Veins** carrying **threft**. Skilled people can brast/veld Veins to avert **harrow**. Deepest Veins (the **Undercurrent**) are strongest near the Bore.

---

## 2) Geography & Capitals
### 2.1 Hemispheric Order
- **The Underface** (Tempered Realms; “Forge half”): semi-dark lands sloping toward the Bore’s red glow.
- **The Outer Crown** (Breathwright Domains; “Glass half”): bright lands refracting sun via high-air panes; frequent lightfall and mirage storms.
- **The Curtain:** ring of violent updrafts and storm shear girdling the transitional latitudes.
- **Riftlands:** shattered terrain between capitals with chasms and glassy scars.

**Distance Units**
- **Chain** = 20 paces  
- **Span** = 10 chains  
- **Field** = 10 spans (≈ a large sport-ground)  
Rift widths are measured in **Fields**.

### 2.2 The Six Capitals (each governs 3–4 satellites)
**Underface (Tempered Realms)**
1. **Grindheim** — basalt terraces, river-forges; guild seat of the Ember Crown.  
   Satellites: Redwater, Blackgate, Slagline.
2. **Cinderfell** — crater city near Bore rim; alloy markets, hazard crews.  
   Satellites: Ashmere, Vent’s End, Brim.
3. **Hammerpost** — canyon bridges, foundry schools; military logistics.  
   Satellites: Irontide, Rivet, Quarry Holt.

**Outer Crown (Breathwright Domains)**
4. **Strathmoor** — tiered glass domes; Collegium of Flows.  
   Satellites: Clearfen, Lumenport, Sandreach.
5. **Aurelion** — sun-lens farms; oath registries; perfume-salt trade.  
   Satellites: Brightfold, Prism Vale, Salt Step.
6. **Veslara** — cliff arches, wind organs; medica of Leesa.  
   Satellites: Glisten, Threadbay, Whisper Ridge.

**Routes**
- **Seam Roads** (dangerous cross-Curtain caravans)
- **Skylines** (tethered glider lanes in the Crown)
- **Gorgeways** (chain bridges and ferries in the Underface)

---

## 3) Society & Guild Systems
### 3.1 Neutral Guild Families
- **Veinwrights’ Conclave** (standards, audits)
- **Surveyors’ Chain** (maps, rift-measure, bridge charters)
- **Mercers’ Table** (weights, coin assay)
- **Couriers’ Bell** (sealed dispatch across Curtain)
- **Healers of the Line** (Leesa-leaning Vein care)

### 3.2 Principle-Aligned Guilds (respectable)
- **Xilcore:** Stonebinders; Alloy Collegium; Rulewrights (geometry law)
- **Blemo:** Purifiers (water, grain, rites); Oathkeepers (contract courts); White Kilns (fine glass)
- **Leesa:** Joiners (masonry unions); Roundhouse Medica (public hospitals); Breadwardens (stores)
- **Seeri:** Salvagers’ Ordinate (reuse); Final Measures (funerary); Rotgardeners (soil renewal)

### 3.3 Fringe & Heresies (gray/violent)
- **Quietists** (philosophy of stillness; “Quieting” as virtue)
- **Shatterhands** (political vandals; Unline to “reset”)
- **Bright Zeal** (Blemo extremists; “burn the taint”)
- **Form Absolutists** (Xilcore extremity; geometry over people)
- **Open Rot** (nihilist Seeri splinter; forced decay)

---

## 4) Religion & Public Rites
### 4.1 Temples & Sigils
- **Xilcore Houses** (hex flags; tool consecrations)
- **Blemo Baths** (ablutions; oath ledgers)
- **Leesa Rounds** (circles for vows and reconciliation)
- **Seeri Cloisters** (mortuaries; compost terraces)

### 4.2 Festivals (shared calendar)
- **Forge-Round** (Underface new year; first iron pour)
- **Clearwake** (Crown spring; first flawless pane)
- **Joining Day** (city amnesties and reconciliations)
- **Return to Soil** (autumn; Seeri thanksgiving)

### 4.3 The Quietist Debate
- **Claim:** the universe trends toward **Quieting**; the gods’ quarrel delays peace.
- **Orthodox response:** Quiet is symptom, not virtue; motion and form sustain life.
- **Status:** tolerated in the Crown; watched in the Underface.

---

## 5) Craft/Magic Mechanics (Vein Theory)
### 5.1 Capabilities
- **Brasting** (force-correction): set load-paths, steady spans/crowds, reinforce oaths.
- **Velding** (flow-guidance): control cool rates, divert heat “like breath,” ease negotiations, knit fractures.
- **Bridging:** pair Veins (teams, couples, beams) to share threft.
- **Measured Unline:** controlled break to reset bad lines (licensed).

### 5.2 Costs & Risks
- **Fray marks:** fine pale striations; tremor; breath-short; ear ring.
- **Hollowing:** erratic judgment; overwork temptations; touch-seeking.
- **Harrow events:** stampedes, bridge failures; always track **load, heat, will**.

### 5.3 Training Track
- **Apprentice → Journeyman → Warden → Master → Assessor** (see §17). Dual-trained exist, but licensure is typically single-path to prevent abuse.

### 5.4 Taboos
- **Blood-brast** (forcing a living Vein under oath)
- **Glass-gelding** (permanent Unline removing a person’s capacity to bridge)
- **Unlicensed mass-work** during festivals

---

## 6) Economy & Technology (Renaissance tipping to proto-industrial)
- **Power:** bellows, water wheels, wind towers, lens furnaces.
- **Transport:** chain-bridges, ore-sleds, sand-skiffs, tethered gliders (Crown).
- **Black powder:** quarry/signal/ceremonial only.
- **Printing:** woodblock + early movable type; contract ledgers common.
- **Combustion studies:** **Fire-engines** (sealed pistons) in Cinderfell labs; experimental; not field-deployed in Series 1–2.
- **Currency:** **Marks** (iron), **Crowns** (silver), **Lumen** chits (rare, near Bore).
- **Trade:** ore/tools (Underface) ↔ glass/salts/medica (Crown).

---

## 7) Civic Systems
### 7.1 Law
- **Capitals Compact:** six capitals pledge road safety, fair weights, extradition of saboteurs.
- **Guild Courts:** craft disputes; temple courts handle oaths.
- **Vein Statutes:** all mass-work logged; wardens may halt unsafe builds.

### 7.2 Measures
- **Distance:** Chain / Span / Field
- **Weight:** Stone / Bar / Load
- **Heat:** **Bell** count (forge); **Shade Steps** (0–12) for glass
- **Time:** Day = **Bell-cycle** (eight named bells) • Week = **Hand** (5 days) • Month = **Turn** (6 Hands) • Year = **Round** (12 Turns) • Current: **Round 612 AS**

### 7.3 Calendar Highlights
- **Borewatch** (equinox): both hemispheres mark the same twilight surge.
- **Oath-Renewal** (mid-year): Blemo rites; contracts re-inked.

---

## 8) Political Blocks
- **Tempered Triad** (Grindheim–Cinderfell–Hammerpost): pro-Xilcore law, controlled Seeri salvage, skeptical of Quietism.
- **Crown Collegia** (Strathmoor–Aurelion–Veslara): Leesa public health, Blemo oaths, tolerant of debate.
- **Seam Markets** (neutral): black/gray hubs at Curtain crossings; Conclave jurisdiction.

---

## 9) Character Dossiers (public, in-world)
> Ages and specifics are guide rails; adjust for scene needs but keep arcs consistent.

### 9.1 Jhace of Grindheim
- **Origin:** Grindheim, Underface
- **Craft:** Veinwright (brast-dominant), bridgewright schooling
- **Traits:** disciplined, plain-spoken, instinct for cleavepoints
- **Faith:** Xilcore customary rites; respects Seeri pragmatics
- **Goal (public):** keep spans standing; keep crews alive
- **Flaw (observed):** over-correction; takes others’ load until fray
- **Ties:** Luthen (former master), Conclave assessors

### 9.2 Tiffani of Strathmoor
- **Origin:** Strathmoor, Outer Crown
- **Craft:** Veinwright (veld-dominant), glass medica research
- **Traits:** incisive, patient, improvisational; reads “bubbles” in systems
- **Faith:** Leesa rites; ethical Blemo oaths
- **Goal (public):** codify safe cool-rates for civic glass and public health
- **Flaw (observed):** rejects “good enough” when a purer solution exists
- **Ties:** Collegium of Flows, Roundhouse Medica

### 9.3 Brighlee (Aurelion)
- **Role:** lens-smith’s apprentice turned courier (Couriers’ Bell)
- **Craft:** veld-adjacent; exceptional at bridging group intent under pressure
- **Traits:** upbeat, logistical genius, refuses to panic
- **Arc:** becomes movement lead across the Curtain

### 9.4 Sha’niah (Cinderfell)
- **Role:** hazard-crew warden; Bore-rim responder
- **Craft:** brast professional; reads heat by breath and foot feel
- **Traits:** blunt, steady, quietly protective
- **Arc:** anchors Underface operations; teaches safe brast under shock

### 9.5 Valari (Veslara)
- **Role:** medica-joiner; Leesa-trained healer specializing in Vein care
- **Craft:** veld healer; pioneers fray-mark treatments
- **Traits:** calm, exacting, unafraid to deny unsafe work
- **Arc:** moral center; enforces rest; rejects blood-brast

### 9.6 Chloen (Grindheim)
- **Role:** Surveyors’ Chain scribe/cartographer
- **Craft:** neutral; maps cleavepoints and rift dynamics
- **Traits:** observant, dry wit, loves ledgers
- **Arc:** proves maps prevent harrow as surely as hammers

### 9.7 Kylar (Strathmoor)
- **Role:** Oathkeepers’ militia escort; Aurelion Registry detachment
- **Craft:** oath discipline; tactical small-load brast for crowd control
- **Traits:** loyal, rules-first, quietly brave
- **Arc:** bends rigid oaths toward merciful outcomes

### 9.8 The Auditor (office)
- **Definition:** civil office within the Conclave; audits Vein logs, certifies mass-work, investigates harrow
- **Powers:** halt orders; seizure of unsafe sites; cross-border writs
- **Perception:** necessary but resented
- **Note:** an office with rotating officers, not a singular figure

---

## 10) Visual Symbols & Color Keys
- **Xilcore:** iron/black, hex marks
- **Blemo:** white/clear, drop sigils
- **Leesa:** green/gold, circles
- **Seeri:** brown/rust, thorn spiral
- **Quietists:** grey cloth bands; blank circle pin
- **Conclave:** blue stamp with crossed chain and quill

---

## 11) Safety Protocols (world logic to respect)
- Any mass-work in public must be logged with the **Veinwrights’ Conclave**.
- Festivals increase threft; no unlicensed brast/veld in crowds.
- Fray is cumulative; characters cannot “push through” indefinitely.
- Blood-brast is a crime in both hemispheres.
- Crossing the Curtain requires a **Seam Writ**; storms can erase maps weekly.

---

## 12) Political Case Studies (one per capital)
### 12.1 Grindheim
**Governance.** Guild-municipal council chaired by the **Ember Crown** Warden; standing seats for Conclave and Stonebinders.  
**Power blocs.** Ember Crown; Stonebinders; Salvagers’ Ordinate; Oathkeepers tribunal.  
**Disputes.** Furnace rations; bridge-rights over Blackgate Gorge; scrap claims.  
**Levers.** Furnace ledgers; rivergate tariffs; Conclave halt orders.  
**Cross-border.** Trades iron bar for Crown salts/medica; skeptical of Quietists.  
**Field guidance.** Wear Conclave blue when brasting; post a Bell-runner; never Unline a canal wall without writ.

### 12.2 Cinderfell
**Governance.** Tri-warden: Alloy Collegium, Hazard Crews, Surveyors’ Chain.  
**Power blocs.** Hazard Crews; Alloy labs; Couriers’ Bell (exclusive route).  
**Disputes.** Lab safety vs quotas; courier exclusivity; salvage rights after light-surges.  
**Levers.** Bore-rim access; hazard stipends; drill closures.  
**Cross-border.** Pragmatic trade in alloy recipes for salts and sand.  
**Field guidance.** Heat-shade cloaks (Steps 8–12); log all work; if light turns violet, clear to second lines.

### 12.3 Hammerpost
**Governance.** Marshal-Magistrate; canyon logistics hub.  
**Power blocs.** Bridgewright Corps; Rivet Quarter Syndics; Final Measures.  
**Disputes.** Tolls; weight limits; funerary route priority post-collapse.  
**Levers.** Toll exemptions; Gorgeway access; burial allocations.  
**Cross-border.** Suspicious of “airy” Crown goods.  
**Field guidance.** Stamped load sheets; tool inspection; demo brast required for foreign wardens.

### 12.4 Strathmoor
**Governance.** Collegium Council; First Provost by seniority.  
**Power blocs.** Collegium of Flows; White Kilns; Roundhouse Medica.  
**Disputes.** Research secrecy vs safety; pane export quotas; medica formula access.  
**Levers.** Kiln schedules; publication licenses; apprentice lists.  
**Cross-border.** Trades lenses/medical ware for alloy; tolerates Quietists if clinics undisturbed.  
**Field guidance.** Announce veld intent; no sand-throwing without sandmaster; apprentices observe.

### 12.5 Aurelion
**Governance.** Civic Senate; Oath Ledgers central.  
**Power blocs.** Oathkeepers’ Registry; Lens-Farm Syndics; Mercers’ Table.  
**Disputes.** False-oath cases; light-rights; coin shortages during storm-season.  
**Levers.** Oath festivals; lens angle decrees; embargoes.  
**Cross-border.** Active diplomacy with Grindheim; largest Seam Market.  
**Field guidance.** Ledgers straight; random bond checks; have an Oathkeeper announce small-load veld for crowd calm.

### 12.6 Veslara
**Governance.** Elder Council; Leesa medica and Joiners dominant.  
**Power blocs.** Roundhouse Medica; Joiners’ Circle; Wind-Organ Conservators.  
**Disputes.** Clinic triage in plagues; union protections vs emergency works; organ preservation vs expansion.  
**Levers.** Clinic access; union endorsements; rite schedules.  
**Cross-border.** Bridges to Hammerpost; medica↔stone expertise exchange.  
**Field guidance.** Enforce Warden rest; never interrupt organ rites (also a crowd-safety signal).

---

## 13) Field Kits & Tool Glossaries
### 13.1 Veinwright (Brast) Kit — Underface
Hammers (face/cross-peen/rounding, 1–3); wedges/dogs; tongs; feeler irons; heat bell cards; quench vessels (oil/water/brine); line chalk (iron-red); ledger & Conclave seal; leather hood; bracers; bell whistle; optional Xilcore tool-string.

### 13.2 Veinwright (Veld) Kit — Crown
Blowpipes (short/long); punty rods; marvers/paddles; blocks/jacks; anneal sand (Shade Steps 3–9) & cradles; shade gauge; thread-clamps; line ink (pale gold); sleeve guards; breath-veil; water ladle; burn salve; kiln ledger; White Kiln stamps.

### 13.3 Surveyors’ Chain Kit
Chains (tagged); plumb lines/levels; sight boards; rift probes; map folio; signal flags; bell-code card.

### 13.4 Couriers’ Bell Kit
Runner satchel; bell pair; writ wallet (Seam, temple, guild); ridge-grip shoes; reversible cloak; rations/salts; rope knife; Conclave pin.

### 13.5 Hazard Crew Kit (Cinderfell)
Heat cloaks (Shade 10–12); hook poles; water lines; breath buckets; stretcher/splints; ward ropes; siren cones.

### 13.6 Medica Vein-Care Kit (Veslara)
Fray salves; cooling stones; pulse bands; needles/thread; clinic tokens; portable quiet screens.

### 13.7 Auditor Inspection Pack
Writ case; measuring rod; inks/seals; witness ledger; badge; witness pins.

### 13.8 Oathkeeper Militia Kit (Aurelion)
Short batons; bond cords; shield boards; horn pair; writ folders.

### 13.9 Seam Market Trader Kit
Neutral stall cloths; dual weight sets; indicator cards; two-key lockbox; weekly safe-exit map.

### 13.10 Bore-Rim Expedition Kit (restricted)
Goggles (violet filter); anchors/lines (glass-bite tips); heat drums; violet tracer chalk; recall flag.

### 13.11 Tool Glossary (selected)
Cleave-iron; bridge-pin; shade hood; bell cards; line ink; fray band.

## 14) Expanded Dossiers & Population Culture
### 14A. Chloen (expanded)
**Function:** Surveyor-scribe (Surveyors’ Chain)  
**Contexts:** rift assessment; crowd guidance; Seam documentation  
**Kit:** sight board; chain; flags; oilskin folio; Conclave ribbon  
**Risk:** low–moderate; no entry beyond second flag line  
**Interpersonal:** dry wit; ledger-first; debt-averse  
**Limits:** weak swimmer; dislikes heights  
**Reputation:** “the ledger that keeps bridges up”

### 14B. Kylar (expanded)
**Function:** Oathkeeper escort (Aurelion) stationed in Strathmoor  
**Contexts:** market disputes; escort duty; oath breach arrests  
**Kit:** batons; shield board; horns; bond cords; writs  
**Risk:** moderate; holds line until medica/Conclave arrive  
**Interpersonal:** formal, literal; soft with elders/apprentices  
**Limits:** over-trusts stamped papers; benefits from Chloen’s scrutiny  
**Reputation:** “the rule that doesn’t bruise”

### 14C. Population Culture (for scene texture)
**Underface**  
- Rhythm: early bells; breaks at Second/Fifth; taverns after Seventh  
- Street foods: skillet breads, iron-pot stews, brine pickles, slag sweets  
- Etiquette: tools are personal; brief eye contact; one-nod thanks  
- Crowd: follows strong voice; respond to whistles/hammer on iron  
- Superstitions: three taps before first pour; coin on new bridges  
- Festivals: Forge-Round; Return to Soil; Bridge Namings  
- Law: respect clear rules; loathe “paper puzzles”  
- Slang: “Hold your line.” “All bell, no heat.” “Lay a bridge.”

**Outer Crown**  
- Rhythm: paced by Shade Steps; long midday rest  
- Street foods: flatbreads, citrus salts, chilled sweets, seed brittle  
- Etiquette: announce intent; hands visible; share water at thresholds  
- Crowd: parts for shade gauge; follows chimes and hand signs  
- Superstitions: never praise flawless glass; leave a lucky bubble  
- Festivals: Clearwake; Joining Day; Oath-Renewal  
- Law: contract-first; mercy expected when truth is plain  
- Slang: “Mind your shade.” “Too clear by half.” “Give it breath.”

**Seam Markets**  
- Mixed crews; Conclave blue visible; armed peace by custom  
- Prices in Marks or Crowns; weights checked by Mercers’ booth  
- Signals: three-bell roll = hazard right-of-way; red aisle flag = halt trade  
- Risks: pickpockets; rumor stampedes; Shatterhand sabotage  
- Norms: no proselytizing; no militia colors; disputes to Witness Table

**Crowd Management Patterns**  
- Underface rule of six: one warden per six carts; bell runner every third corner  
- Crown spiral: clockwise flow around incidents; never into glare  
- Shared signal: open palm = stop; crossed wrists = route blocked; two fingers forward = move slow

**Family & Apprenticeship**  
- Underface: apprentice by Second Round; public guild marks often override surnames  
- Crown: choose House after Trial Week; family names on oaths  
- Marriage: temple or Oathkeepers; practical dowries (tools, shop shares)  
- Funerary: Underface returns tools and ashes to Seeri beds; Crown wraps white cloth, inters with etched pane

**Speech & Address**  
- Craft: wright, warden, assessor, master, apprentice  
- Civic: provost, marshal, elder  
- Religious: keeper (Blemo), round-mother/father (Leesa), binder (Xilcore), reader-of-ends (Seeri)  
- Honorifics: Goodhand, Goodmind

**Rumor Ecology**  
- Underface: ore wagons; tavern singers; Conclave postings at dusk  
- Crown: broadsheets at dawn; Collegium boards midday; clinic chime-gossip  
- Seam: “nothing is true until Mercers stamp the weight”

---

## 15) Case Materials (exemplar documents)
### 15.1 Conclave HALT ORDER
Header, writ number/date, site, cause (Harrow risk), order (suspend), custody (tools locked), compliance window (one bell), seals (blue/red/black), witness marks. Back page checklist: ledger review, load test, bell count, fray check.

### 15.2 Oathkeepers’ BOND LEDGER LEAF
Parties, object, term, penalty, escape clause (Curtain storms), countersign, receipt slips.

### 15.3 Clinic TRIAGE SHEET
Craft exposure; fray signs; cooling/heating injury; treatment path; return-to-work; keeper mark.

### 15.4 Seam WRIT
Bearer/crew; authority; goods/weights; valid routes; storm clause; void conditions.

### 15.5 Salvage CLAIM
Site + sketch; type; hazard; mitigations; cleric mark; expiry.

---

## 16) Map Insets & Symbols
### 16.1 Lines
Primary road (double solid); Gorgeway/Bridge (bold with ticks); Seam route (dashed with triangles); **Heat main** (thick red with hollow circles); **Glass main** (thin gold with squares).

### 16.2 Sites
Conclave House (blue square + quill); Temple (god sigil in circle); Clinic (green ring); Kiln/Furnace (black square + chimney); Hazard Station (red triangle); Auditor Office (blue hexagon); Market (open grid).

### 16.3 Terrain/Hazards
Rift (heavy jag, width in Fields); Glass-scar (pale gold crackle); Slag flow (brown arrows); Curtain shear (grey hatch + wind arrows); Bore glare (violet stipple).

### 16.4 Flags/Bells
Red flag = halt; Blue = Conclave work; Yellow chevron = fall back.  
Bell codes: single long (clear span); two short (brace); three fast (Harrow imminent); rolling (hazard right-of-way).

### 16.5 Sample Insets
- Grindheim Blackgate Sector (terraces, bridges, heat main junction, slag drains)  
- Strathmoor Kiln Quarter (lens farms, shade alleys, cooling courts, glass mains)  
- Seam Market (witness table, weights booth, neutral stalls, storm exits)

---

## 17) Training Progressions
### 17.1 Veinwright (Brast)
- **Apprentice (6–12 Turns):** hammer control; load math; bell cards; Harrow drill. Safe: 1 Field repairs (Warden present).
- **Journeyman (12–24):** pier resets; controlled Unline; crowd-steady protocols. Safe: 2 Field projects; supervise 2 apprentices.
- **Warden (exam):** bridge lifts; mass-work logs; halt authority. Safe: 3 Field jobs.
- **Master:** complex spans; multi-crew coordination; Conclave liaison.
- **Assessor:** audits; certifies; issues halt orders.

**Health rails:** Fray bands; two Bells rest after Harrow; clinic if bands pale twice in a Hand.

### 17.2 Veinwright (Veld)
- **Apprentice:** shade gauge; blowpipe; anneal; hand-sign crowd spacing.
- **Journeyman:** large panes; molds; dual cooling lanes; ledger timing.
- **Warden:** kiln schedules; emergency cool; purity liaison.
- **Master:** structural/clinic glass; field cool during fires.
- **Assessor:** certifies Houses; investigates pane failures.

**Health rails:** Breath-veil for Shade 6+; no solo over Shade 7; Quiet room after veld burns.

### 17.3 Surveyors’ Chain
Apprentice (chain/plumb/ink) → Journeyman (rifts/bell runners/flags) → Warden (sector maps) → Master (cross-Curtain charts).

### 17.4 Hazard Crews
Runner (sirens/ropes/stretchers) → Handler (heat cloaks/hooks/couplers) → Lead (triage/perimeters/violet retreat).

### 17.5 Oathkeepers
Deputy (bond basics/baton/witness) → Officer (riot lines/small-load brast/mediations) → Registrar (ledger audits/forgery).

### 17.6 Conclave Auditors
Inspector (document checks/tool seizure/photosketch) → Assessor (halt authority/cross-border writs/inquiries).

---

## 18) The Thermal Mains (“Heat Pipes”)
Ancient, city-spanning sealed conduits carrying steady heat; present in every capital and most satellites. **No credible record** shows installation; predates Round 0 AS.

### 18.1 Names
Official: **Thermal Mains** (Mains) • Colloquial: **Heat-lines / Deep Runs / Redmains** • Street: “**warmbones**”.

### 18.2 Observed
Dark matte alloy; seamless welds; ring bands every **Span**; trunk ~1 chain diameter; feeders ~½; laterals ~¼. Constant hand-warm; hotter on seasonal surges. Faint ticking at Bell changes. Access collars exist but fasteners do not match known threads.

### 18.3 Layout
Trunk loop in each capital; feeders to kilns, clinics, baths, greenhouses, winter shelters. Bridge-district bypasses (Underface). Anneal court stabilizers (Crown).

### 18.4 Operation (public practice)
Valves with open/close levers (city-coded). Legal taps at civic sites; private taps require Conclave permit. No known pumps; flow self-equalizes. Outages rare; mains re-warm unaided.

### 18.5 Governance
Conclave custody of maps and junction keys; cities hold easements. **Mains Law:** no cutting/boring/unlicensed taps; violations seized to Oath court; blue flag at any access.

### 18.6 Maintenance Protocols
Inspection (temp checks; valve tests; leak spotting); surface bracing; collateral stonework. Forbidden: grinding; welding; bite-clamps; Unline within 1 Chain.

### 18.7 Hazards/Signs
Weeping heat (mark yellow; notify); Red-ring (clear area; rolling bell); Rattle (feeder mis-set); Violet haze (evacuate; Hazard Crews).

### 18.8 Theories (public, unproven)
Divine works; pre-Severing artifice; Bore feed; Quietist “cooling veins.” Conclave prints no official stance.

### 18.9 City Integration (examples)
Grindheim (baths, kitchens, infirmaries; trunk skirts foundries); Cinderfell (hazard depots, shelters); Hammerpost (canyon-lip mains; half-closed bypasses); Strathmoor (anneal courts; White Kilns monitor purity drift); Aurelion (oath halls winter heat; lens farms avoid mains); Veslara (clinics; organ humidifiers).

### 18.10 Mapping & Symbols
Trunk = **thick red line** with hollow circles every **Span**; feeders = thin red with solid dots; junction IDs like **GH-R12**.

### 18.11 Field Add-Ons
Thermo-slips; non-bite clamps; listening cone; valve keys (city-coded; Conclave holds masters).

### 18.12 Incident Playbook
1) Spot (yellow chevron) • 2) Clear two Fields • 3) Notify Conclave + Hazard (rolling bell) • 4) Test thermo-slips x3 • 5) Set valves as instructed (never force) • 6) Log and seal; reopen only on Assessor order.

### 18.13 Cultural Notes
Underface: “warmbones”; festival taps. Crown: clinic stability; household feeders in cold Turns. Seam: no private taps—license revoked if found.

---

## 19) Conclave Inquiry Templates (Glass-and-Iron Practice)
### 19.1 Inquiry Flow (on-site)
1) Stabilize (blue flag, clear two Fields, Witness Pair) → 2) Log → 3) Sketch → 4) Interview → 5) Test → 6) Decide.

### 19.2 Witness Q&A Tree
Identity; task intent; controls; load/heat/will; deviation; signals; aftercare.

### 19.3 Photosketch Layout
Plan view; elevation; hazard overlays; Thermal Main trace.

### 19.4 Evidence Chain
Blue seals, tool tags, samples, shard wraps, oath copies.

### 19.5 Failure Classes
F-1 planning; F-2 material; F-3 procedure; F-4 external; F-5 sabotage.

### 19.6 Sample Findings
Boilerplate reopen/deny text.

### 19.7 Sanction Ladder
Admonish → Fine → Suspension → Seizure → Temple/Oath referral.

---

## 20) Bore-Rim Operating Rules (Cinderfell Codex)
### 20.1 Hazard Scale (Violet Index)
V-0 clear → V-4 storm.

### 20.2 Crew Composition
Lead Warden + Shade Warden; Survey Pair; Hazard Six; Bell Runner; Witness Pair.

### 20.3 Retreat Patterns
Single-file rope; alternate bridge; headcount and clinic bands.

### 20.4 Muster & Sirens
Amber cone brace; violet cone withdraw; rolling bell clears lanes.

### 20.5 Work Windows
Two Bell-cycles after dawn and before dusk; no equinox field work without writ.

### 20.6 Equipment Rails
Shade hoods; violet goggles; non-bite clamps; no powder stores on rim.

### 20.7 Common Incidents
Slag creep; pane wind; heat weep at mains.

---

## 21) Civic Iconography Pack (Banners, Seals, Street Marks)
### 21.1 Color Standards
Iron Black, Forge Rust, Pane Gold, Clinic Green, Oath White, Conclave Blue, Quiet Grey, Bore Violet.

### 21.2 Banners & Marks
House banners for Xilcore, Blemo, Leesa, Seeri; Conclave yard; Seam market neutral.

### 21.3 Wayfinding Icons
Thermal access, clinic, auditor, hazard, witness table, quiet room.

### 21.4 Permit Stamps
Stall, street work, procession writ.

### 21.5 Hand Signals
Stop, slow, block, spiral.

---

Curtain Shear, Lightfall belts, Gloom Drift; Dry/Haze/Storm/Clear Turns; city notes; weather work advice.

Domestics/workbeasts; wilds; plants/materials; foodways; hazards/taboos.

Fuels; kitchens; drink; clothing; housing; street hardware; daily tools; festivals; waste/repair.

Sound tendencies; names; by-names; toponyms; address; oaths; proverbs; profanity; writing conventions.

Schooling; path choice; apprenticeship; exams; seals; cross-training; discipline; continuing practice; lectures.

Force types; doctrine; formations; logistics; siege/denial; threats; arrest-to-adjudication.

Offenses; circuits; smuggling; scams; sentencing; how law actually works.
