## 13) Forms & Icons (N4)

- **Drill Card:** lane map, tap cadence, Second Lines arcs (dashed), muster corners (◻), water posts (⧉), clinic (⭕), Conclave yard (◼︎+quill). Bell codes: single long clear; two short brace; three fast Harrow risk.  
- **HALT (partial):** lane/site, cause, tools locked, one-bell compliance, blue seal, two witnesses, **setter must witness clear** line.  
- **Headcount Sheet:** rope-team list, bells checked, clinic referrals, **boss signature** line.  
- **Photosketch:** plan of gullies/lanes/Second Lines, signed by Warden and Assessor.

---

## 14) Production Rails (model do/don’t)

**Do**  
- Stage a full daylight **hazard drill** with real pressure and human friction.  
- Use **V-0 to V-2** cues only.  
- Post **one partial HALT** when warranted and clear it same day with a witnessed stamp.  
- Let **paperwork be the spine** (card, headcounts, photosketch, HALT/clear).  
- Show **cost** every time (fray, rest, time, coin).  
- End chapters on a **provable change** (mark, card update, signature, stamp) with a concrete sensory image.

**Don’t**  
- Don’t introduce violet storms, deep-rim expeditions, barnacle harvests.  
- Don’t access/speculate about Thermal Mains.  
- Don’t add engines, telegraph, firearms as common kit.  
- Don’t turn friction into a courtroom scene; this is drill admin, not a trial.

---

## 15) Chapter Word Count Rails
- Aim **900–1500 words** per chapter; one major operation and one civic consequence per chapter.

---

## 16) Hard Locks Checklist (reject if violated)
- HALT count ≠ 1 or not lifted same day → **reject**.  
- Any Curtain/Bore mechanics beyond ambient mention → **reject**.  
- Thermal Mains access/incident/theory → **reject**.  
- V-3+ procedures or divine activity → **reject**.  
- Taboos depicted/taught → **reject**.  
- Chapter ends without **provable change** → **revise**.  
- POV drift beyond Sha’niah/Jhace (+ brief Brighlee) → **reject**.

---

## 17) One-Page Hand-Off (paste above the drafting prompt)

**POVs:** Sha’niah primary; Jhace secondary; Brighlee brief.  
**Setting:** Cinderfell Bore-facing ward drill yard, depots, clinic spur, Conclave yard.  
**Magic-as-work:** Trace, Set, Pressline, Tempermark, micro-Bind, Veil canopies, Map-as-Set, Second Lines, Countermark (HALT tag/clear).  
**Costs:** fray bands, salts, Quiet Room, fatigue.  
**Paperwork spine:** Drill Card with Second Lines; headcount sheets with boss signature; photosketch; one partial HALT and same-day clear.  
**End state:** refined **Drill Card** posted; **Second Lines** mapped; one partial HALT lifted after fix; depot boss signatures on headcounts; clinic log routine.

This packet gives a drafting model everything it needs to write **Novella 4** cleanly: focused Veinwork, one real HALT, Second Lines doctrine, human stakes, and zero leakage beyond this book.

[USER]
and the summary

[ASSISTANT]
Here’s the full, detailed summary of **Novella 4: Second Lines**. It stays inside this book’s rails: one partial HALT that’s lifted the same day, no gods, no engines/telegraph, Thermal Mains as background warmth, Veinwork as real work with cost.

---

### Dawn: muster, maps, and a yard that hums
Cinderfell’s Bore-facing ward wakes to heat shimmer and dust. **Sha’niah**, the Hazard-Crew Warden, posts a clean **Drill Card**: lane map, bell cues, **Second Lines** arcs for retreat, muster corners, water posts, clinic spur. Rope-teams gather at **siren cones**; **Brighlee** chalks headcount boards and pins water placement notes leeward of prevailing gusts. A **Conclave Assessor** arrives with seal box and a measuring rod, not impressed by speeches, only by prepared yards.

**Jhace** is the visiting brast/bridgewright. He’s there to observe and suggest improvements, not run the show. He sketches a **brace tweak** for a suspect gantry but agrees with Sha’niah it will be tested on a **dead gantry**, not live. That sets the tone: **try off-line, adopt only with proof**.

First bell: **muster discipline**. Teams form by rope, caller posts marked, map set where eyes actually pass. The yard’s noise levels drop because people know where to stand. Cost is small and honest: grip fatigue, sweat under heat cloaks, nothing dramatic.

---

### Morning drills: small wins that matter
A faint **V-1** tick glints in a slag skin at the rim. Sha’niah **Traces** the shimmer, lays **yellow chevrons** across a lane to narrow flow, and marks the **clinic** “on alert.” The drill proceeds, but the yard leans away from that skin as if it has instincts.

Hook work begins. On two-short bells, crews **Pressline** and **set hooks** to steady a training gantry. Jhace times taps to a **long breath** instead of a frantic arm; the shudder calms. He logs the cadence on the card: “short taps, long breath.” Forearms burn. A few **fray bands** shift pale; that’s why the clinic screen is up in the shade.

A **depot boss** pushes for an early reopen of a narrowed lane. Sha’niah says no, out loud, to his face, in front of the Assessor. The Assessor writes “review” instead of “breach,” which in this world is as good as applause. The drill keeps running.

---

### Logistics pressure and the first near-miss
Gusts cut across the yard; heat rises. **Water posts** go dry too soon. Brighlee moves them **leeward** and counts heads again. Two names appear to be missing. She sprints the boards to Sha’niah, who orders a **recount at Second Lines**. The “missing” crew was mis-filed under a different rope. Correction logged. A chalk **circle** goes up on the headcount board: “No one lost.” It’s bureaucratic poetry and it matters.

The **dead-gantry** trial for Jhace’s brace tweak runs behind the yard. With **Pressline** and a quick **Tempermark** on a pin, the bell card’s **load curve flattens** noticeably. Skeptical locals nod once. No adoption yet, but the proof is pinned next to the card.

---

### The partial HALT: fence, not theater
Midday heat intensifies the earlier tick into a **sustained V-2** worry on a live lane that overlooks a slag gully. Sha’niah orders the Assessor to post a **partial HALT** on that lane only. Blue **lockout tags** go on tools; a bold **public note** names the cause: “Harrow risk; chevrons insufficient; fix plan pending.” The HALT reads like a fence, not a scolding.

A crew member’s **fray band pales** to near white. The **Clinic Medica** pulls them to the **Quiet Room** for one bell, issues a **paid-rest slip** (per posted ordinance), and rebalances rotations so the gap doesn’t tempt shortcuts. Compassion fatigue creeps in; still, the yard runs.

**Photosketch time**: Sha’niah and Jhace align a fix plan on paper—photosketch of gullies and braces plus Jhace’s annotated cadence. The Assessor won’t lift the HALT on good feelings; the plan gets a reopen **target bell** chalked on the board. Everyone can see the homework.

---

### Re-test and reopen: stamps that mean something
Under the next two bells, they trial the correction on the once-iffy lane: **short taps, long breath**; **Pressline** light; **Set** clean. The yard listens to itself. The training gantry holds with a steadier profile; the slag skin keeps its distance like a dog that’s been ignored. The **Assessor** requires the **setter** of the HALT to **witness the clear**; Sha’niah does. The **blue seal** sinks flat. The **HALT lifts**. People exhale.

Brighlee chalks in the **Second Lines** arcs and **muster corners** as refined by the morning’s flow. Children in the viewing gallery trace the dashed arcs with fingers and repeat the caller’s words like a game. That’s the point of a public drill: doctrine becomes playground.

---

### Afternoon: headcounts, signatures, and the boss who learns
Final passes prioritize **headcount integrity**. Rope-teams run to corners by muscle memory. The depot boss returns to mutter about throughput. He’s handed a revised **headcount sheet** with a new **signature line** (courtesy of policy work that no one names on-page). He signs that **drill windows outrank cargo** during V-1/V-2 conditions. It’s not a confession. It’s a binding.

Throughout, **Thermal Mains** sit where they always sit: warm air near the clinic and depot vents, valve wheels under lock, no access, no speculation. The book points its camera at people doing work that keeps other people alive.

---

### Dusk: a city learns a sentence
At dusk the **refined Drill Card** is posted for real: chevrons painted and numbered, tap cadence noted, **Second Lines** mapped, muster corners boxed, water posts repositioned, clinic spur arrowed, **“one hand on home line”** carried over for any Bridges, and a simple sentence at the bottom that functions as doctrine: **“Second Lines save lives.”** The yard is quiet in the good way: tired bodies, checklists with ink drying, tools where they belong.

**Clinic log** shows routine numbers: salts, rests, one Quiet Room hold, returns filed with next-shift bells. No Harrow, no heroics. Just a yard that will be safer tomorrow because the paper and the muscle agree.

---

### Micro-politics that make it stick (felt, not sermonized)
- Elsewhere, **Corlexi** standardizes the Drill Card plate; weeks later other cities will use the exact layout and people will think it was obvious.  
- **Mobel**’s **boss signature line** appears on headcount sheets, turning a habit into accountability.  
- **Salee**’s one-line placard, **“Pale fray triggers paid rest. Do not argue,”** is posted at clinic and depot. Arguments end before they begin.  
- **Eries** posts a dawn **retirement schedule** for old gantries with a bounty; dangerous hardware quietly disappears.

None of that is a speech in this novella. It’s the background hum of a city deciding not to be stupid.

---

### Emotional throughline (kept small, adult)
Jhace and Tiffani exchange brief **letters** via Brighlee—one line each, tucked under the logistics: a cleaned phrasing margin from him, a tidy symbol key from her. In the yard, Jhace quotes **her rest ethic** aloud once to back Sha’niah’s Quiet Room pull. That’s it. The romance remains subordinate to the drill, which is why it reads as real.

---

### Concrete outcomes you can point at tomorrow
- **Second Lines doctrine** is mapped, posted, and legible; children can find corners without a caller.  
- **Partial HALT** toolchain works like a fence, not a stunt: posted cause, tagged tools, fix plan on paper, **setter witnesses the clear**.  
- **Tap cadence** and **chevron placement** are codified on the card.  
- **Water posts** are repositioned leeward by rule; **headcount** cadence formalized.  
- **Boss signatures** are required on headcount sheets during drills.  
- **Paid rest** for pale fray is posted and enforced.  
- The yard closes **without Harrow**; the clinic log reads routine.

**Net effect:** a hazardous ward practices surviving itself and writes the practice down so it will work when nobody important is watching. The city ends the day slightly harder to kill.

Alright, here’s your **single, self-contained Novella 5 Bible + Beat Sheet**. It’s built to pivot the series from grounded civic magic into the first unmistakable brush with the bigger thing… without cheating. It assumes nothing outside this novella exists for the drafting model. No gods onstage. No engines. Thermal Mains stay wallpaper. One faint, unnamed Breakwake ripple happens once, exactly when Tiffani dies saving Jhace from a human-made “accident.”

# TE’Oga • NOVELLA 5 BIBLE + BEAT SHEET (Innocent, Standalone)

**Title stub:** Festival Lines  
**Timeframe:** Round 612 AS, Turn 7 (festival day + dusk)  
**Primary POVs:** Jhace (brast/bridgewright), Tiffani (veld/clinic-aligned)  
**Brief POVs allowed:** Brighlee (logistics), Chloen (scribe/photosketch), Kylar (escort), Valari (medica), Assessor (Conclave)  
**Scope locks:**  
- No Bore deepwork. No Curtain crossings. No engines/telegraph/guns-as-kit.  
- No divine interventions or prophecies.  
- **Thermal Mains**: background heat only; no access, incidents, or theories.  
- **Exactly one Conclave HALT** may be posted and must be lifted same day (lift occurs after the incident fix).  
- Death on record: clinic attempt, Conclave forms, witness marks.  
- One faint, **unnamed** phenomenon at impact second (the future “Breakwake” seed). No one names it here.

---

## 0) World Basics (usable in this doc)
- **World:** **Te’Oga**, pierced hemisphere, irrelevant beyond climate.  
- **Civic physics:** All things carry **Veins** with **threft** (built pressure). Skilled operations Set/Bridge/Veil/Pressline, etc., to avert **Harrow**.  
- **Public ideals:** Xilcore/Form, Blemo/Purity, Leesa/Wholeness, Seeri/Decay. Rites exist; no miracles on-page.  
- **Measures & Time:** Chain (20 paces), Span (10 Chains), Field (10 Spans). 8-bell day.

---

## 1) Setting Slice — Festival Corridor & Blackgate Approaches
- **Texture:** bunting lines, lane cloths, temporary risers, bell posts jammed with ribbons, jar vendors, lens carts, food steam, escort ropes, clinic tent moved forward for surge, extra water posts, Conclave **Witness Table** tucked at the corner.  
- **Institutions present:** Conclave yard team (Assessor + seal box), Mercers’ Table (weighs), Couriers’ Bell, clinic medica, escort ropes (Kylar), steward consortium reps.  
- **Friction today:** throughput vs safety, counterfeit authority in the crowd, hardware fatigue on a festival-stressed span.

---

## 2) Veinwork: Operational Lexicon (N5 scope)
**Shared fundamentals:** **Vein**, **threft**, **cleavepoint**, **Field**, **Bridge**, **Unline** (not executed today), **Fray**, **Hollowing**, **Harrow**.

**Underface (forge) moves used:**  
- **Trace** (read span pins/brace lines), **Set**, **Pressline** (pulse through stall braces to steer push), **Tempermark** (lock a replacement pin), **Bind (micro)** (pair braces at chokepoints), **Countermark** (clear HALT).

**Outer Crown (glass) moves used:**  
- **Lume** (quick jar/lens reads), **Veil** (shade canopy to cool crowd Veins), **Thread** (save a running hairline), **Slake** (staged cool + clinic rest cadence).

**Field moves:**  
- **Map-as-Set** (icons/arrows), **Bridge (crowd)** under bells, **Confiscation** as civic Countermark for fake ribbons.  
- **Taboos:** blood-brast, glass-gelding, unlicensed mass work.  
- **Prohibited today:** Overbind across unlike systems.

**Costs:** fray bands pale, tremor, jaw/shoulder burn, Quiet Room time. Paid rest is policy.

---

## 3) Law, Safety, Paper (N5)
- **Festival Placard:** lane arrows, bell codes, crowd breaks by bell, wedge-before-Veil, “one hand on home line,” **ribbon audit box** (new), **pin custody seal** (new), clinic position addendum.  
- **Conclave HALT:** writ with site/lane, cause, tools locked, compliance one bell, blue seal, two witnesses, **setter must witness clear**.  
- **Incident Log:** site, bell, injuries, actions, witness marks, **footnote space** (Assessor can note anomalies without naming them).  
- **Confiscation Notice:** counterfeit ribbons seized, cause, corner, custody.

---

## 4) Thermal Mains (background only)
- Hand-warm culverts near clinic bath and food stalls. That’s it. No access, no failures, no blame.

---

## 5) Tools & Kits
- **Brast:** hammers, wedges/dogs, feeler irons, bell cards, line chalk, lockout tags, rivet tongs.  
- **Veld:** shade cloths, poles, line ink, jar cradles, breath-veils, anneal sand, burn salve.  
- **Escort:** rope lines, sleeve whistle, **ribbon-check card**.  
- **Conclave:** writ case, blue seals, custody seals, witness ledger, confiscation tags.  
- **Clinic:** fray bands, cooling stones, salts, splints, Quiet screen.

---

## 6) Cast Dossiers (N5-allowed)
**Jhace** — brast/bridgewright. Calm under bells; over-shoulders risk.  
**Tiffani** — veld/clinic-aligned. Anneal timing, Veil discipline; perfectionist honesty.  
**Brighlee** — logistics. Keeps water, headcounts, postings moving.  
**Chloen** — scribe. Photosketches, seal checks, incident entries.  
**Kylar** — escort. Crowd read; ribbon fakery spotter.  
**Valari** — medica. Enforces paid rest; pulls pale bands.  
**Assessor** — Conclave. Will post and clear HALT as law requires.  
**Steward Fixer** — consortium go-between; coin-first; smiles too much.

---

## 7) Romance Track (adult, consent-clear, never during bells)
- **Early private scene:** behind a locked storeroom door pre-festival. Urgent, consensual, hands on back/neck, fade. They check fray bands after.  
- **Public code:** an innocuous phrase embedded on the festival placard line they co-edited.  
- **No performative grief during duty.** The loss lands after the paperwork, which hurts worse.

---

## 8) Political Micro-Interludes (tiny, end on artifacts; no “conspiracy” speeches)
- **Corlexi** adds a **ribbon audit box** to the placard and bolds “paper before coin.” *Artifact:* updated placard plate.  
- **Mobel** introduces **pin custody seals** for span hardware; wrong ink or missing seal = no install. *Artifact:* custody seals at the Conclave table.  
- **Salee** moves the clinic tent **onto the flow** and posts a **hot-day rest ladder**. *Artifact:* clinic placement addendum card.  
- **Eries** schedules overnight **retirement** of a gantry spec flagged during festival stress. *Artifact:* dawn ledger “Removed.”

---

## 9) Summary Spine (for the model’s head)
A festival day ramps pressure. Crowd volume exposes two frauds at once: **fake authority ribbons** and a **tampered span pin**. The fixer’s plan is to misroute a lane and pop the pin while Jhace resets a brace. **Tiffani sees it, body-checks Jhace aside, takes the crush, and dies.** At the impact second, there’s a faint, unnamed **one-beat synchrony**: bells align, chalk dust lifts, straps settle. Then it’s gone. The city documents the death, confiscates the ribbons, seals the hardware chain, posts a refined standard, lifts the HALT. Jhace doesn’t glow; he works, signs, and breaks later (not here).

---

samarry please

[ASSISTANT]
Here’s the full, detailed summary of **Novella 5: Festival Lines**. It stays inside this book’s rails: one HALT lifted same day, no gods, Thermal Mains as wallpaper, Veinwork as real work with cost, and Tiffani’s death on the record. The single strange moment is an **unnamed** one-beat synchrony that no one explains.

---

### Dawn: the city tightens its laces
Festival day. Bunting everywhere, crowds already sweaty and impatient. A unified **festival placard** goes up at the Blackgate approaches and the clinic front: lane arrows, break bells, “paper before coin,” **ribbon audit box**, **pin custody seal** icon, wedge-before-Veil glyph, “one hand on home line.”  
- **Tiffani** tunes the **Veil** canopies over glass rows, staging shade crews and cool benches.  
- **Jhace** walks the span approaches and **Traces** deck hardware; one pin’s grain ring looks wrong in a way only a brast cares about. He notes “replace after next bell” and flags the steward.  
- **Chloen** lays out incident forms and the **Witness/Confiscation** kit; **Brighlee** sets water posts and a break schedule board.  
- **Kylar** quietly checks ribbons at the chokepoints with a weave card.  
- **Valari** moves the clinic tent forward so care intersects the heaviest aisles.

### The screw turns: audit, custody, pressure
Second bell swells into the first surge.  
- Tiffani orders **wedge-before-Veil** on a drafty corridor, preventing early hairlines.  
- Jhace runs **Pressline** pulses at two corners: short taps, long breath. Crowd flow goes from jerky to human.  
- **Mobel’s** new **pin custody seals** arrive at the Conclave table. Any pin installed today must carry a sealed ID with witness marks. Stewards grumble; paper doesn’t care.  
- **Kylar** catches a near-blue **fake ribbon**. He doesn’t lecture; he **confiscates** under notice and nails the paper at the corner. Crowd mood: skeptical, then compliant.

Behind the scenes, a slick **steward fixer** keeps smiling and pushing for “throughput.” He wants braces pulled early and that suspect pin left “for later.” Jhace shuts him down with posted math: delay cheaper than closure, right there in numbers a child can follow.

### The trap arms
By mid-day the crowd’s a living river. The fixer’s plan staggers two small cheats:
1) a counterfeit ribbon reroutes a lane tighter than the placard allows;  
2) a **pre-scored pin** waits to shear when a brace is reset.

Jhace returns to the flagged bracket with a replacement pin and a rivet set. He intends to swap it after the next bell, per his own note.

**Assessor** preps a **HALT writ** just in case, every field filled except time. Not drama. Good practice.

### The shear and the shove
The counterfeit ribbon flashes at the east lane just as Jhace steps to **Pressline** and reset. The lane compresses wrong, the **tampered pin** shears early, and the crowd’s loaded vector snaps straight at him. It happens in three breaths.

**Tiffani** is close enough to understand the trajectory. She **body-checks** Jhace out of the crush path, throws a single **Veil cue** to slow the front rank, and takes the impact on her side between a cart and stone. It’s physics, not poetry. Bones. Breath. Silence following a sound that doesn’t belong at festivals.

The **HALT** drops instantly. Blue tags go on tools. Clinic screens come up. **Valari** and two runners try to stabilize her. Chloen’s quill scratches open the **Incident Log**: site, bell, sequence, actions, injuries.

### One beat that isn’t normal (and that’s all)
While Jhace is on the cobbles, hand on chalk, there’s a single **unnamed** beat of… agreement. Bells along the approaches **tick as one**, chalk dust **lifts** like an inhale, straps **settle**, and the front of the surge **stills** instead of tripping. Then it’s gone. Jhace blacks out for a blink and comes back shaking. No one names it. The Assessor adds an **anomaly footnote** in dry ink and moves on because the city is bleeding time.

### Fix the thing; lift the fence
Duty doesn’t pause for heartbreak.  
- Jhace and the crew **Tempermark** a vetted replacement pin, install it with proper **custody seals**, and re-square the brace.  
- Lane leads **Bridge** the crowd with clear bell calls, each crew keeping one hand on its home line.  
- The **Assessor** requires the **setter** of the HALT to **witness the clear**. The stamp sinks flat. Fence down. Flow resumes, muted and careful.

**Kylar** finishes ribbon sweeps; the satchel of fakes sits under custody with a notice nailed where people can see it. No bravado. Just consequences.

### Paperwork and grief, in that order
**Valari** logs the clinic attempt and the time of death. **Chloen** closes the **Incident Log** with witness marks from two neutrals and the Assessor’s footnote about “one-beat synchrony observed; source unclear.” The **festival placard** is replaced with a refined version that includes the **ribbon audit box** and **pin-custody** line as doctrine, not suggestions. The board is **Tempermarked** so rain can’t warp it. Vendors start copying the math to their own walls. People who hate rules still follow these ones.

Only then, away from bells and eyes, the crew breaks. Brighlee’s hands won’t stop moving. Jhace’s won’t stop shaking. The book gives them space without speeches. No divine whispers, no vow to conquer fate. Just the blunt fact that Tiffani is gone and the city is safer because she was there.

### Dusk: the world that remains
At dusk, a single long bell. Lane cloths settle. The corner where it happened holds. The **HALT** is resolved in the ledger. The **confiscation notice** stays posted. The refined **festival card** is up in two places, one on each side of the Quiet Street. Water posts are still full. The clinic log reads what it reads.

**Concrete outcomes you can point at tomorrow**
- **Ribbon audits** become standard; fakes are seized under notice.  
- **Pin custody seals** required for span hardware; wrong ink or missing seal means “no install.”  
- **Break bells** and **wedge-before-Veil** are printed, not oral tradition.  
- **One HALT** was posted and lifted with the setter as witness; the process works.  
- **Incident Log** includes a plain-language sequence, clinic outcome, and a tiny **anomaly footnote** no one is allowed to build a religion on.  
- The festival **placard** now encodes what saved lives today: audit, custody, clear bell phrasing, home-line discipline.

**Emotional net:** two professionals who loved each other like adults spent a day doing their jobs. One died saving the other. The city learned rules it will keep. Somewhere inside one man the ceiling quietly moved, and nobody in this book knows what to call that.

[USER]
now a single auditor epilogue for this novella 5

[ASSISTANT]
# Epilogue: Auditor’s Minute, Blackgate Festival — Round 612 AS, Turn 7

**File:** CONC-612/7-BG-FEST-AFT  
**Office:** Veinwrights’ Conclave, Oversight Desk  
**Auditor of Record:** A. Therrow  
**Witness Marks Attached:** K. Verdin, P. Holl

---

## Scope

Review of a crowd-management failure and corrective action at the Blackgate approaches during festival load. This minute records sequence, artifacts, deviations, and adopted standards. It closes with a sealed note regarding an observed irregularity. No conjecture is entered; only what was seen, timed, measured, or signed.

---

## Sequence, Bell by Bell

**First bell:** Festival placards posted at span and clinic fronts. Placards included break-bell blocks, lane arrows, “paper before coin,” “one hand on home line,” **ribbon audit box**, and **pin custody seal** icon. Water posts charged. Clinic tent advanced to intersect main aisles. No objections recorded.

**Second bell:** Shade crews deployed **Veil** canopies above glass row; wedge-before-Veil upheld. At east and south corners, brace pulses (**Pressline**) used to loosen forming knots; cadence annotated as “short taps, long breath.” Forearm fray observed in crew leads; clinic salts issued. **Mobel custody seals** on span hardware accepted by installing parties.

**Before third bell:** Escort lead **Kylar** confiscated near-blue authority ribbons in violation of placard rule (“paper before coin; Conclave blue obeyed”). **Confiscation Notice** posted at southeast corner. Crowd temperature dropped to routine skepticism.

**Third bell:** Brast lead **Jhace** flagged a suspect deck pin on east approach, grain ring presenting off-center “shear omen” under trace. Noted: “replace after next bell.” Steward representative urged delay of replacement beyond that window. **Cost sheet** posted; “delay cheaper than closure” read aloud. No further argument entered.

**Between third and fourth bell:** Assessor prepared **HALT writ** with blanks for time and lane. Standard practice during festival surge.

**Fourth bell + small:** Misrouting attempt at east lane executed by an unknown with counterfeit ribbon; lane compressed beyond placard tolerance. At brace reset, suspect pin **sheared early**. Load vector struck toward brast lead.

**Impact instant:** Veld lead **Tiffani** body-checked brast lead out of the crush path, issued a single Veil cue to the front rank, and took the force between cart and stone. Clinic screen up within breaths; medica **Valari** applied care. **HALT** dropped; blue tags applied; tools locked. **Incident Log** opened; sequence recorded on the line.

**The breath after:** Bells along the approaches struck as if from one hammer; chalk lifted from cobbles and hung; straps and lines settled; the crowd’s fore rank steadied instead of folding. This interval lasted one beat and no more. Brast lead reported brief blackout, recovered to function. No further effect measured. Logged below as **Irregular Observation 1**.

**Fourth bell + long:** Replacement pin, previously vetted and sealed, installed and **Tempermarked**. Lane leads **Bridged** flow with bells to decompress corner; each crew kept one hand on its home line. **Assessor required setter’s witness**; HALT cleared; stamp bit flat.

**Dusk:** **Refined festival placard** posted, incorporating ribbon audits and pin-custody lines as doctrine. Confiscation notice remained. **Clinic ledger** closed with outcome. Witness marks taken.

---

## Artifacts Entered

- Festival Placard, Rev. F (ribbon audit box and custody icon added; Tempermarked board)  
- Custody Seal Ledger: five issued, four installed, one held; ink true  
- Confiscation Notice: seven counterfeit ribbons seized; custody to Kylar; corner SE-2  
- Incident Log: sequence, actions, injuries, clinic outcome, witness marks  
- HALT Writ: site/lane East-Blackgate; cause “Harrow risk”; set/clear on record, setter witness line filled  
- Cost Sheet (public): delay vs closure numbers countersigned by steward rep

All artifacts legible. Ink, wax, and marks verified.

---

## Deviations and Faults

1) **Counterfeit authority** in a surge environment. Source unconfirmed. Placard audits prevented wider error.  
2) **Hardware tampering** (pre-scored pin). Custody seals adopted same day; no further questionable installs observed.  
3) **Lane misroute** contrary to placard arrows. Correction achieved by escort ropes and bell calls; no militia colors displayed, as required.

Responsibility remains under local registry inquiry. This office records only the procedural effect: the three faults were contained by placards, custody, confiscation, and one HALT.

---

## Costs

- **Personal:** Veld lead **Tiffani** deceased on record. Clinic attempt documented; time of death entered by medica. Pale fray bands across multiple crew observed and treated; Quiet Room holds issued as needed.  
- **Civic:** One HALT posted, cleared same day with setter witness. Temporary stall closures at corners SE-2 and E-1 during fix.  
- **Material:** One failed pin removed; replacement installed. Confiscated ribbons out of circulation.

---

## Standards Adopted Same Day

- **Ribbon Audits:** spot checks at chokepoints using weave exemplars; counterfeit ribbons confiscated under notice.  
- **Pin Custody:** sealed identification on all festival-day hardware installs; wrong ink or missing seal equals no install.  
- **Break Bells & Wedge-First:** printed blocks for breaks; wedge-before-Veil codified.  
- **Home-Line Discipline:** “one hand on home line” retained; brace tap zones grey-striped at corners.

These appear on Rev. F placard and are enforceable by posted text and public habit.

---

## Irregular Observation 1 (Footnote)

> *At the impact second, bells aligned across separate posts; chalk lifted and hung as if in the draw of one inhalation; straps settled of their own accord; the front of the surge steadied without a shouted command. Duration: one beat. No aftereffect measurable. Source unclear.*  
> — A.T., Auditor

No label assigned. This office recommends no public language beyond what is written above. The record stands for future correlation if similar beats are reported.

---

## Closing Note (Sealed; opened only on written request from Oversight)

I am trained to distrust eloquence. Good minutes are small, built of times and marks. Still, some things resist the ledger. The crews obeyed their own card. The crowd learned a sentence in one day. And between a cart and a stone, a worker paid a price that bought the rest of us another morning.

We have posted the board in two places, one on each side of the street. People will point to lines and pretend lines saved them. Lines are just how we remember the hands.

— **A. Therrow**, Auditor of Record

One city pair only: **Grindheim** and **Strathmoor**.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1 World Basics\n+\n+### 1.1 Cosmology (public)\n+\n+- **World:** **Te’Oga**, a wounded sphere.  A historical strike pierced one hemisphere, leaving a planet‑spanning tunnel called **the Bore**.  This event is irrelevant on‑page here.\n+- **The Four (public ideals):**  **Xilcore/Form** – structure and geometry (vice: rigidity); **Blemo/Purity** – refinement, oaths, cleansing (vice: zeal); **Leesa/Wholeness** – healing and joining (vice: smothering unity); **Seeri/Decay** – endings, compost, renewal (vice: rot for its own sake). tools locked; resolved same day.  \n+- **Witness Pair:**  Two neutral marks to settle disputes or verify tests.  \n+- **Clinic slip:**  Arrival, exposure, fray signs, treatment, return bell, keeper mark.  \n+- **Work cards:**  Icon‑first instructions with bell cues, lane arrows, slake intervals, wedge‑before‑veil glyph, and “one hand on home line.”\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5 Thermal Mains\n+\n+Dark matte trunks warm clinics, baths, and shelters. Attraction is tied to craft.  \n+2. **Proximity test:**  They stand shoulder‑to‑shoulder over a map, elbows brushing.  “Close?”  “Stay.”  \n+3. **Charged touch:**  She guides his wrist to set bell tempo; he steadies her at the hip when a vendor jostles.  \n+4. **Confessional slip:**  After a HALT, adrenaline high, one admits, “When you called that, I wanted you.”  “Likewise.”  They table it until dusk.  \n+5. **Private kiss:**  At dusk they find a quiet corner and share one hard kiss.  They agree: never during bells, never at a worksite.  \n+\n+Consent cues: “Close?” “Stay.” “Yes.” “Stop?” “Don’t stop.”  They keep professional titles in public; first names in private. A sagging market span threatens to fail.  **Jhace** traces the crossed Veins twice—wet traces lie—and posts a blue flag against the vendors’ fury.  He Tempermarks a replacement pin with a down‑breath strike, sets the deck, and logs the halt.  His fingers tremble from cold and fray.\n+\n+Across the world in **Strathmoor**, **Tiffani** fights hairlines in a clinic corridor.  She Veils the run and logs that her bench timing fails in real air.  She wedges the draft, rewrites her note for floor hands, and suffers a headache.  The White Kilns grumble; she doesn’t budge.  **Brighlee** carries these notes between cities.  Attraction begins not with eyes but with respect for honest ledgers.\n+\n+As the novella unfolds, Jhace and Tiffani exchange corrections (redlines, half‑beat slake intervals) and adopt each other’s improvements.  They thread cracks, countermark old pride, bind braces during a halt, and test a new bell‑call cadence.  Their romance is rooted in competence.  They make time for one private kiss at dusk; they agree no intimacy during bells.  **Fray cost** is always on page: pale bands, tremor, Quiet Room rests.\n+\n+Behind the scenes, small clerks make the world better: **Corlexi** pares a safety notice down to one page; **Mobel** refuses a fraudulent stamp; **Salee** stocks clinics; **Eries** removes a hazard at night.  These micro‑politics add up to a culture shift.\n+\n+The final trial run sees both cities cooperating.  Flags align, bells echo a new cadence, veins are set across a **Field** of people.  The test ends with a posted circular co‑signed by both shops.  No Harrow occurs.  Children repeat bell calls like a game.  The world changes by paperwork and breath.  The novella closes with Jhace walking the quiet span in rain and Tiffani writing “Injuries: 0” in her ledger.  They have built a rule that will save lives later.\n+\n*** End Patch\nEOF\n"]}

The story stays in the forge‑dark **Underface** and the bright **Outer Crown**; the cataclysmic Bore itself is only distant background.  Days are divided by bells, distances by chains, spans and fields.  Everything – metal, stone, flesh, crowds – has an internal **Vein** that carries **threft** (pressure).  Skilled workers manipulate Veins to prevent **Harrow**, the catastrophic release of threft.  Magic reads like engineering: actions have names, costs and legal rails.\n+\n+* **Magic verbs (N1 scope):**\n+  * **Trace** reveals a hidden Vein with chalk, heat or quench steam.\n+  * **Set** aligns a Vein with taps or clamps before force is applied.\n+  * **Pressline** pushes threft along a chosen path to steer weight or crowds.\n+  * **Tempermark** locks an alignment using a heat–cool cadence; leaves a faint ring on metal.\n+  * **Bind (micro)** permanently joins two small structures; illegal **Overbind** across systems is forbidden.\n+  * **Lume** shows a line in hot glass; **Veil** lays a cooling skin that guides inner lines.\n+  * **Thread** stitches a narrow Vein across a fracture in glass.\n+  * **Slake** drops threft by staged cooling; **Annealshift** changes the resting path of a Vein across an entire piece.\n+  * **Countermark** negates a previous mark (used once here).  All actions cost **fray** (pale bands, tremor, breath), and serious work requires rest or clinic care.\n+\n+* **Locations:**\n+  * **Grindheim (Underface)** – basalt terraces, forges, bell posts, canal and span districts.  Issues: furnace rations vs market schedules.\n+  * **Strathmoor (Outer Crown)** – tiered glass domes, kiln quarter, shade alleys, cooling courts.  Issues: purity quotas vs clinic safety.\n+\n+* **Legal and paper:** the **Veinwrights’ Conclave** monitors public work and can post a blue‑seal **HALT** to stop unsafe operations.  Papers matter: notes, ledgers and standards become public doctrine.  Taboos (blood‑brast, glass‑gelding, unlicensed mass work) are illegal but not explained.\n+\n+### Cast\n+\n+* **Jhace** – bridgewright-trained brast warden from Grindheim.  He feels lines, over‑corrects and carries others’ burdens.  Values: “crew comes home,” Xilcore’s tool law.  Flaw: self‑sacrifice.\n+* **Tiffani** – veld warden from Strathmoor, attached to clinics and kilns.  Brilliant at timing anneals, improvising cooling paths and writing procedures in plain language.  Flaw: perfectionism.  Values: compassion, truth in ledgers.\n+* **Brighlee** – courier who keeps schedules, water posts and messages moving.  Over‑commits but is logistical glue.\n+* **Chloen** – Surveyors’ Chain scribe; maps cleavepoints and keeps ledgers accurate; risk‑averse.\n+* **Corlexi**, **Mobel**, **Salee**, **Eries** – clerks glimpsed in interludes.  They trim notices, refuse bad stamps, quietly fund safety supplies and schedule removals.  Each scene ends with a public artefact (a card, cost sheet, stock ledger or removal record).\n+\n+### Culture\n+\n+Underface speech is short and clipped; Crown speech open and formal.  Idioms include “Lay a bridge,” “Mind your shade,” “All bell, no heat.”  Food is skillet breads, stews and citrus‑salted sweets.  Respect for tools and ledgers is deep: you never touch another’s hammer or misrecord a load.  Public safety is a civic duty, not heroism.\n+\n+### Interludes\n+\n+Four single‑scene interludes show background politics:\n+\n+1. **Corlexi** simplifies a safety notice into a single bell‑call chart that anyone can read.\n+2. **Mobel** rejects a steward’s exception stamp with wrong ink; the brace stays in place.\n+3. **Salee** quietly re‑allocates budget to buy extra fray bands and water bowls for clinics.\n+4. **Eries** schedules overnight removal of a condemned awning.  Each interlude ends with a tangible, posted artefact.\n+\n+### Romance track (N1)\n+\n+Jhace and Tiffani are drawn to competence.  They trade respectful notes, guide each other’s hands during work and share one hard, private kiss at dusk.  They set clear boundaries: no touching during bells, no public displays, and consent is always verbal.\n+\n+### Learning ladder\n+\n+Novella 1 takes the characters through basic Veinwork:\n+\n+1. **Trace** in rain; learn that wet traces lie, so trace twice.\n+2. **Veil** a corridor; wedge the draft before veiling.\n+3. **Pressline** short pulses with long breaths to calm a crowd; permit brace taps on bells 2–3.\n+4. **Annealshift** with a three‑count at corners.\n+5. **Tempermark** a replacement pin on the down‑breath.\n+6. **Countermark** a bad fix; cooler than the birth temperature.\n+7. **Slake** on half beats; half‑beats cut failures.\n+8. **Thread** a live crack; oil guides Thread.\n+9. **Set** a Field with flags and caller posts.\n+10. **Bind (micro)** door braces; warm touch points are stable.\n+11. **Map as charm** – icons beat words.\n+12. **Combined Veil/Pressline/Bridge** under bells; keep one hand on home line.\n+13. **Countermark** HALT; the setter must witness the clear.\n+14. **Tempermark** boards so rain doesn’t warp them.\n+15. **Slake the body** – rest is part of work.\n+\n+## Beat sheet\n+\n+There are 15 short chapters.  Each ends on a concrete change to the world or the characters:\n+\n+1. **Rain on Blackgate** – Jhace traces a sagging span in rain, posts a blue flag and refuses to rush.  The halt slows trade but prevents a collapse.\n+2. **Shade Six, Not Seven** – Tiffani’s new cooling schedule works in the kiln but fails in a corridor.  She writes it down honestly.\n+3. **Ledger Threads** – Jhace and Chloen realise their maps differ; he sketches a corrected aisle plan and notes that paper must follow practice.\n+4. **Clinic Glass** – Tiffani threads a splint in a drafty corridor and logs that door wedges are needed.\n+5. **Three Bells to Speak** – Jhace refuses a market steward’s demand for speed; a cart tips, bruises two, and he promises to write procedure.\n+6. **Proof, Not Pride** – Tiffani drafts a plain one‑page anneal note; Brighlee carries it to Grindheim.\n+7. **Break It Then** – Jhace stress‑tests her note in rain; writes a respectful redline; failure becomes shared knowledge.\n+8. **Lines that Breathe** – She revises again, kills jargon and adds a late‑bell clause.  White Kilns grumble but stamp the trial.\n+9. **Witness Pair** – Conclave schedules a live test with a witness pair; flags and bell runners are posted.\n+10. **Blue Flag, Green Chits** – During the trial a signal tangles; Jhace posts a HALT, and Tiffani orders rest for a pale‑band apprentice.  Vendors protest but no one dies.\n+11. **Measure Twice, Speak Once** – Chloen redraws choke points; Tiffani rewrites the call‑and‑response for bells so floor hands can remember it under stress.\n+12. **Two Short, One Long** – They run the full trial; crowd flows without a shove; the witness marks the minutes.\n+13. **Hands to Tools** – The Assessor lifts the HALT with a single stamp; no applause, just relief.\n+14. **Posted at Dusk** – The joint circular goes up with arrows, bells, Slake intervals and wedge symbols.  Children mimic the calls.\n+15. **Lines That Hold** – Jhace walks the span at night and hears only water and distant chimes.  Work resumes tomorrow; sentiment stays off the tools.\n+\n+## Summary\n+\n+**Lines That Hold** follows two warden‑craftspeople on a mundane yet transformative week.  In Grindheim, **Jhace** traces a rain‑soaked span and posts a blue‑flag halt, incurring vendor wrath but averting disaster.  In Strathmoor, **Tiffani** slogs through cracked clinic panes, invents new cooling protocols and records failures honestly.  Through exchanged notes carried by **Brighlee**, the pair refine each other’s methods: half‑beat Slaking, wedge‑before‑Veil, short pulses and long breaths.  **Chloen**’s conflicting maps force them to treat paper as a living charm, not gospel.  They stage a live test with Conclave observers, posting flags, bell runners and a witness pair.  When signals tangle during the trial, Jhace posts a HALT and Tiffani orders rest, proving that procedure beats pride.  After revising bell calls and lane arrows, they run a flawless trial.  A joint safety card – half icon, half prose – goes up at span and clinic, Tempermarked so rain cannot warp it.  In private, Jhace and Tiffani share one urgent kiss and promise to keep passion separate from public work.  Children echo the new calls.  The novella closes with a quiet span and a ledger entry reading “Injuries: 0.”  No gods stir; no prophecies are uttered.  The miracle is that a city chooses to learn.\n+\n*** End Patch\nEOF"]}

Below are the five requested Bible/beat‑sheet/summary combinations for Novellas 1–5 as individual `.md` documents. Each code block contains the complete reference information, chapter‑end beat targets, and an extensive summary for that novella.

### **Novella 1 Bible + Beat Sheet + Summary (`novella1.md`)**

```markdown
# Novella 1 — Lines That Hold: Bible & Beats & Summary

## World Basics
- **Setting:** Te'Oga — a planet pierced by a tunnel (“the Bore”) through one hemisphere, creating the underlit Underface and the sun‑bright Outer Crown.
- **Physics / Magic:** Everything contains **Veins**, lines of structural strength carrying **Threft** (latent strain). Skilled workers detect and adjust Veins using brast/veld craft to prevent **Harrow** (collapse).  
- **Terms:** Trace, Set, Pressline, Tempermark, Bind, Lume, Veil, Thread, Slake, Annealshift, Countermark. Costs include **Fray** (pale bands) and **Hollowing** (post‑work fatigue).

## Cast & Roles
- **Jhace** – Brast‑dominant veinwright from Grindheim; calm, detail‑oriented, sometimes self‑sacrificing.  
- **Tiffani** – Veld‑dominant veinwright from Strathmoor; perfectionist, improvisational, medica‑aligned.  
- **Brighlee** – Energetic courier managing messages, maps, and logistical details.  
- **Chloen** – Surveyor scribe producing maps and seal checks.  
- **Corlexi / Mobel / Salee / Eries** – Minor interlude clerks who quietly fix bureaucracy (placard design, seal enforcement, clinic stocking, hazard retirements) and post new policies.

## Culture & Interludes
- Underface slang is clipped; Crown diction is formal.  
- Food: skillet breads, stews, citrus‑salt sweets.  
- Interlude snapshots show how mundane clerks like Corlexi simplify procedures, Mobel rejects unsafe stamps, Salee shifts budgets for fray bands, and Eries quietly removes hazards; each ends with a posted notice that changes public behavior.

## Romantic Track
- Respect sparks attraction: they fall for each other’s competence.  
- Early touches are incidental (guiding a wrist during a bell count); later, there is one private kiss in a shadowed alley after a successful procedure.  
- They avoid public intimacy during work; consent is explicit (“Close?” / “Stay.”).

## Learning Sequence (Magic)
- **Trace** wet beams to reveal hidden Veins in rain.  
- **Veil** corridors by laying damp cloths to cool glass.  
- **Pressline** pulses steer market crowds.  
- **Annealshift** adjusts splints by staggered movements.  
- **Tempermark** locks a span pin via heat.  
- **Countermark** removes an unsafe old mark.  
- **Slake** half‑beat cooling prevents cracks.  
- **Thread** stitches a running hairline in glass.  
- **Bind** braces a door; **Map-as-Set** uses icons to drive crews.

## Beat Sheet (15 Chapter End Targets)
Each chapter ends with a concrete change and sensory image:
1. **Blackgate Trace:** Trace sagging deck, post blue flag. Rain beads cling to chalk lines.
2. **Shade Six Veil:** Wedge drafty door, veil cools the glass. Gloss dulls to safe matte.
3. **Pressline Underfoot:** Brace pulses calm crowd push. Puddle rings ripple in rhythm.
4. **Annealshift Corridor:** Staggered set‑down ends hairline. Crack stops crawling.
5. **Tempermark Pin:** Down‑breath strike fixes pin. Faint ring catches lamplight.
6. **Countermark Mercy:** Cooler‑than‑birth heat erases a bad fix. Old mark dulls to gray.
7. **Slake Revised:** Half‑beat cool saves glass. Steam curls in repeating loops.
8. **Threaded Crack:** Oil‑guided thread stops hairline. Vein halts like it reached shore.
9. **Field Set:** Flags, bells and caller align crews. Flags twitch together on still air.
10. **Micro Bind:** Bind braces at clinic entrance. Creak stops in unison.
11. **Map-as-Charm:** Icon map adds arrows; workers follow. Smudged arrow forms a perfect curve.
12. **Flow Under Bells:** Combined Veil/Pressline/Bridge works. Knot of carts loosens itself.
13. **Reopen & Countermark:** HALT lifted with witness. Blue seal sinks flush.
14. **Posting Rite:** Tempermarked board posted. Smoke ribbon climbs then vanishes.
15. **Quiet Span:** Work done; river whispers. Jhace pockets the chalk stub.

## Summary
Rain leaks through Grindheim’s Blackgate span. **Jhace** traces hidden Veins under planks and halts traffic to avoid a collapse. In Strathmoor’s clinic corridors, **Tiffani** redesigns anneal timing with a wedge‑before‑veil rule to stop hairlines in glass. Couriers—especially Brighlee—carry memos back and forth; small fix‑notes travel faster than gossip.

Across the first half of the novella they revise each other’s procedures. Jhace redlines Tiffani’s cooling card; she tweaks his bell phrasing. Together they produce a one‑page safety card containing wedge‑before‑veil icons, half‑beat Slake intervals, and bell call cadences. Politics happens quietly: a placard is simplified, a false exemption stamp is refused, budgets shift to buy fray bands, a condemned awning disappears.

The story climaxes with a test of the new standards on market day. A blue flag halts the span when signals tangle; Tiffani binds clinic door braces and adds a “touch check” rule; Jhace ensures the crowd’s load is shared. After a final live trial, the Conclave lifts the HALT. Both cities post identical boards at span and clinic; children repeat the bell calls like songs. In shadow, Jhace and Tiffani share a single urgent kiss then go back to their separate homes. The novella ends not in fireworks but with quiet—the city safer, the workers trusted, and the knowledge that love can grow through craft.
```

### **Novella 2 Bible + Beat Sheet + Summary (`novella2.md`)**

```markdown
# Novella 2 — Glass Breath, Iron Oath: Bible & Beats & Summary

## World & Craft Basics
- **Focus Setting:** Grindheim bridge district (Underface) and Strathmoor kiln/clinic quarter (Outer Crown).  
- **Magic/Veinwork:** Same physics as Novella 1, with added techniques: **Measured Unline** (fenced operation to break and re‑route stressed lines), **Countermark** (erase an old Tempermark), **Bind** on small assemblies (door braces), **Slake** (staged cool), and continued use of Trace, Pressline, Veil, etc.  
- **Costs:** Fray (pale bands), Hollowing (fatigue), mandatory clinic rest after heavy work.

## Cast & New Roles
- **Tiffani** – More engaged with medica duties; pushes for safe cool rates over quotas.  
- **Jhace** – Confronts steward pressure; explains cost-of-delay to market bosses.  
- **Valari** – Medica joiner enforcing paid rest for fray.  
- **Brighlee** – Courier, now coordinating cross‑city schedules.  
- **Chloen** – Scribe doing photosketches and map integration.  
- **Corlexi, Mobel, Salee, Eries** – Interlude clerks adjusting forms (Unline writ fence line, public cost posts, paid rest slips, retirement boards).

## Culture & Interludes
- Two capitals refine their collaboration. Interludes highlight civic upgrades: map thumbnails for Unline writs, math posted to kill false economy, rest slips for paid recovery, bounty boards to retire dangerous tools.

## Romance Track
- Physical closeness escalates: they lock a door and make out urgently before a major operation. They treat back rubs as decompression. Consent is explicit, with a private vocabulary hidden in cards they share publicly.

## Learning & Escalation
- **Corridor delta testing:** Real‑air vs bench results encourage public admission of failure.  
- **Measured Unline:** Fenced cut on flawed training panes; requires perimeter and witnesses.  
- **Cost math:** Jhace uses coin logic to end steward arguments; cost sheet posted.  
- **Rest ethic:** Paid rest slips tie fray to wages, normalizing rest.  
- **Slake half‑beats:** She revises her cooling schedule to half-beats, saving many pieces.  
- **Plain-language cards:** They translate procedures into icon cards for illiterate floor workers.

## Beat Sheet (15 Chapter End Targets)
1. **Heat Takes Its Time:** Tiffani admits corridor failure; writes bench‑versus‑real note.
2. **Brace or Break:** Jhace posts delay logic; steward must sign cost sheet.
3. **The Walk Test:** They track failure patterns instead of hiding cracks.
4. **Measured Unline:** Tiffani proposes a fenced Unline for training panes.
5. **Clinic Chime:** Valari sets triage flows; rest slips appear.
6. **Mark the Cut:** She draws the Unline path and learns cooler‑than-birth temperature rules.
7. **Bell Cards Don’t Lie:** Jhace re‑routes his rehearsal to respect her test window.
8. **Blue Flag, Calm Hands:** HALT posted; door braces micro-bound; ring is stable.
9. **Cut, Lift, Breathe:** The Unline works; steam curls in repeating loops.
10. **The Steward’s Math:** Cost sheet countersign ends pressure for speed.
11. **One Sheet Workers Remember:** A one‑page icon card emerges, accessible to everyone.
12. **Field Proof:** Combined span/trade flow works under festival load.
13. **Reopen:** HALT lifted; the fix stands.  
14. **Posted in Two Cities:** The same card goes up in both cities.
15. **Iron Oath:** They sign off; world continues; no heroism.

## Summary
This novella explores how cross‑city procedure becomes common practice. **Tiffani** wrestles with cool rates in Strathmoor’s corridor; she logs failure as data instead of hiding it. **Jhace** fights market stewards who pressure him to pull braces early; he proves with math that delays cost less than disasters. They propose and execute the first **Measured Unline**, fenced with a defined perimeter; the fix rewrites stressed glass to safe lines while everyone watches. The operation is supervised by a Conclave Assessor and uses paid rest slips; Valari ensures workers rest when fray appears.

Interludes show civic shifts: forms get fence lines; public math kills bravado; rest slips tie recovery to wages; bad tools are retired before they kill. As the trial runs succeed, icon cards replace paragraphs. By the end, Strathmoor and Grindheim post the same one‑sheet standard at their gates: wedge‑before‑veil, half‑beat Slake intervals, one hand on home line. The second novella closes with them tired but whole, having proved the value of procedure and trust.
```

### **Novella 3 Bible + Beat Sheet + Summary (`novella3.md`)**

```markdown
# Novella 3 — Across the Quiet Street: Bible & Beats & Summary

## Setting & Magic
- **Scene:** A single market day at the Seam, a neutral cross‑hemisphere trading district.  
- **Magic:** Operations used include **Map-as-Set** (placards with icons), **Pressline** pulses to steer crowds, **Veil** canopies to cool busy rows, **Lume** to read glass, **Thread** to mend running hairlines, and **micro‑Bind** on braces. Crowds act like Fields; their Veins align under calls and lanes.  
- **Costs:** Fray (pale bands) and fatigue; rest at clinic tent with paid slips.

## Characters
- **Jhace** – Brast, leads lane control; tension with steward after N2.  
- **Tiffani** – Veld, manages glass row and clinic interface; calm under pressure.  
- **Brighlee** – Courier, coordinates schedules, receipts, water posts.  
- **Kylar** – Escort, prevents lane misdirection; checks ribbons authenticity.  
- **Chloen** – Scribe, verifies seals, writes plain‑language rulings.  
- **Vendors, Mercers, Conclave Observers** – Provide friction.  
- **Interlude clerks (Corlexi, Mobel, Salee, Eries)** – Adjust placards, check weights, reposition clinics, post confiscation logs.

## Culture & Law
- **Rules:** No militia colors; paper before coin; lane arrows binding; re‑weighs on request; counterfeit ribbons confiscated; Conclave blue obeyed.  
- **Placards:** Cleaned by Corlexi; unify symbols readable by both sides.  
- **Confiscation Notices & Witness Table:** Provide rapid arbitration; decisions posted publically (refunds, fines, stall closures).

## Romance Track
- Tension flares into attraction; they find intimacy in competence: a hidden palm‑squeeze during a ruling, a kiss behind a tent panel. Public demeanor remains professional.

## Learning & Progress
- Introduces **crowd Field management**: A market can act like a worksite. Fraud detection through tactile weight checks; ribbon audits; serial‑numbered stones. The Witness Table’s plain‑language rulings become de facto law.

## Beat Sheet (15 Chapter End Targets)
1. **Dawn Placard:** Unified icons posted; lane cloths flutter above.  
2. **Glass Row Heat:** She senses a jar’s wrong weight; schedules a re‑weigh.  
3. **Ribbon at the Elbow:** Kylar spots a near‑blue ribbon; blocks misdirection.  
4. **Two-Short, Brace:** Jhace pulses Pressline; puddle rings ripple.  
5. **Stone and Counterstone:** Fraud revealed at re‑weigh; hearing scheduled.  
6. **Receipts Don’t Match:** Brighlee spots duplicate receipts; stores them.  
7. **Cool the Street:** Veil canopy calms the row; sweat turns into sheen.  
8. **Seal Check:** Chloen pins a fake seal sample; wax glints wrong.  
9. **Witness Pair:** Neutral vendors step up; chalk marks their names.  
10. **Confiscation Notice:** Fake ribbon satchel seized under notice.  
11. **No Panic Today:** Rumor dies when Jhace shows a stable corner.  
12. **Table at Fourth Bell:** Hearing yields plain ruling.  
13. **Sign Here:** Parties sign refund schedule; chalk dries.  
14. **Grey Stripe, Clear Flow:** Lane revision posted; cloth hangs straight.  
15. **Market at Dusk:** Placards updated; market closes on one long bell.

## Summary
On a day when Grindheim and Strathmoor trade at the Seam, a placard makes the rules clear: lanes, bells, re‑weighs, audits. **Tiffani** senses a jar lot feels too light and calls for a re‑weigh. **Kylar** spots a counterfeit ribbon and stops an unauthorized lane shift. **Jhace** uses Pressline pulses to calm the first crowd surge. Duplicate receipts and shaved stones hint at organized fraud; these are logged and used at the Witness Table. **Brighlee** manages water and headcounts; her logistics keep the market safe. **Chloen** compares Conclave seals; false stamps are burned.

At the hearing, vendors must restate their wrongs; refunds and fines are posted. A new rule emerges: counterfeit ribbons are seized on sight and noted on a public confiscation ledger. More subtle changes matter: clinic tents shift onto the path, so help walks past trade; paid rest slips tie fray to wages; icon‑first cards replace text.

The day ends with the crowd uninjured and the city slightly wiser. There are no heroics—just clear bells, honest scales, and people choosing not to push. Jhace and Tiffani, pulsing with unsaid feeling, steal a quick kiss behind a canvas panel. They go home tired but alive; the world moves on, safer by increments.
```

### **Novella 4 Bible + Beat Sheet + Summary (`novella4.md`)**

```markdown
# Novella 4 — Second Lines: Bible & Beats & Summary

## Setting & Stakes
- **Where:** Cinderfell’s Bore‑facing ward (Underface) with slag gullies, drill yards, depots, clinics.  
- **Why:** Civic hazard drills train crews to survive real surges; they map **Second Lines**—pre‑surveyed safe arcs where workers retreat and headcount.  
- **Weather:** Heat shimmer, sudden gusts; violet storms (V‑3+) off‑limits.  
- **Magic Tools:** Trace, Set, Pressline, Tempermark, micro‑Bind, Map‑as‑Set (cards), Veil (shade), Slake (rest), Countermark (HALT).  
- **Cost:** Fray bands fade, Quiet Room breaks, jaw and shoulder burn; clinics issue paid rest.

## Cast
- **Sha’niah** – Hazard‑Crew Warden; leads the drill; enforces safety over quota.  
- **Jhace** – Visiting brast; offers a brace‑load improvement but defers to local protocol.  
- **Brighlee** – Courier; runs messages, water, headcount sheets.  
- **Assessor** – Conclave observer; posts and clears HALT.  
- **Depot Boss** – Wants reopen early; signs accountability line later.  
- **Medica** – Runs clinic; checks fray bands, issues rest.  
- **Interludes (Corlexi, Mobel, Salee, Eries)** – Standardize cards, add boss signature lines, mandate paid rest, retire old gear.

## Culture & Protocol
- **Drill Card:** Map with lane flows, tap cadence, Second Lines arcs, muster corners, water posts, clinic spur.  
- **Headcount Sheets:** Names, bells, clinics, mandatory boss signature.  
- **HALT:** Partial; used if a lane shows sustained V‑2; resolved same day after fix.  
- **Rules:** Short taps, long breaths; call and rest intervals; one hand on home line; no powder charges; V‑3/violet procedures banned.

## Beat Sheet (15 Chapter End Targets)
1. **Muster at First Bell:** Sirens gather crews; rope teams hum; map pinned.
2. **Guest With a Ledger:** Jhace’s brace tweak to be tested on dead gantry.
3. **Heat Weep, Chalk Mark:** V‑1 flicker; lanes narrowed; yellow chevrons chalked.
4. **Two Short, Set Hooks:** Pulsed Pressline; hooks catch; shudder steadies.
5. **The Boss’s Window:** Boss pushes reopen; safety outranks coin.
6. **Water and Names:** Gust moves posts; headcount shows “missing” names; rediscovered.
7. **Count Again:** Second Lines check; “No one lost” circle chalked.
8. **Dead Gantry Trial:** Brace tweak tested; curve flattens; adoption later.
9. **Partial HALT:** Sustained V‑2; tools tagged; lane fenced; cause posted.
10. **Quiet Room, Quick Return:** Pale bands pulled; paid rest slip; rotations adjust.
11. **Photosketch and Proof:** Plan aligned; reopen target bell chalked.
12. **Re‑test Under Two Bells:** Hooks set; lane passes; HALT cleared.
13. **Second Lines Posted:** Arcs dashed; muster corners numbered; children trace them.
14. **The Boss Signs:** Accountability signature; new norm.
15. **Dusk Yard:** Drill Card posted; clinic log routine; quiet wins.

## Summary
Cinderfell’s train crews gather at dawn under **Sha’niah**’s eye. The new **Drill Card** maps lanes, flags muster corners, shows Second Lines, and notes cadence (short taps, long breaths). **Jhace** proposes a brace‑load tweak—but not on live loads; the yard tests it on a dead gantry.

Heat shimmer ticks; a slag skin flickers. They chalk **yellow chevrons** to narrow lanes (V‑1). Pulled ropes and rhythm taps steady the gantry; heads bow; breath synchronizes. Workers drop water posts leeward, run headcounts, find missing names on the wrong row, correct them, chalk **“No one lost”** in a circle. No one turns it into a speech.

Mid‑day, a lane hums wrong (**V‑2**). Sha’niah tells the **Assessor** to post a **partial HALT** on that lane. Blue tags go on tools; a note reads “Harrow risk: fix plan pending.” **Valari** pulls a pale‑banded worker into the Quiet Room; a paid rest slip ties rest to wages. **Photosketch and notes** refine the fix. They test the brace tweak later on dead hardware; the curve flattens; adoption is logged. They return to the live lane, fix it under clear bells; the HALT lifts.

Day’s end sees a refined **Drill Card** posted with arcs and numbered corners. Children trace it; adults memorize it. The **depot boss** signs an accountability line that says safety outranks coin under V‑1/V‑2. No violet storms, no heroism, just quiet discipline.

Interludes show small policy moves: Corlexi standardizes the Drill Card layout; Mobel adds signature lines; Salee prints a one‑line “pale band triggers paid rest” slip; Eries retires aging gantries. None of it is flashy, but the city changes: workers know where to go when panic threatens, and signatures stop bosses from shrugging off blame. The novella ends with no Harrow and a drill that reads like a ritual for survival.
```

### **Novella 5 Bible + Beat Sheet + Summary (`novella5.md`)**

```markdown
# Novella 5 — Festival Lines: Bible & Beats & Summary

## Setting & Stakes
- **Festival day:** Blackgate span and clinic approaches throng with celebrants.  
- **New rules:** Placards now include break bells, lane arrows, **ribbon audit boxes**, **pin‑custody seals**, wedge‑before‑Veil, “one hand on home line.”  
- **Physics & Craft:** Veinwork still uses Trace, Set, Pressline, Tempermark, micro‑Bind, Veil, Thread, Slake. **Measured Unline** not used today. A partial HALT may be posted once and must be lifted after fix.  
- **Costs:** Fray bands, fatigue, salts; paid rest is policy.

## Cast & Roles
- **Jhace** – Bridgewright in charge of span safety.  
- **Tiffani** – Veld‑craft leader, assigned to manage heat and cool on glass row and clinic lanes.  
- **Brighlee** – Courier, runs supplies, headcounts, and posts.  
- **Chloen** – Photosketch scribe; logs incidents.  
- **Kylar** – Escort, stops counterfeit ribbon misdirection.  
- **Valari** – Medica; pulls pale bands, logs deaths.  
- **Assessor** – Conclave; posts and clears HALT.  
- **Steward Fixer** – Human antagonist; uses a forged ribbon and an acid‑scored pin to misroute and sabotage.

## Culture & Law
- **Festival placard:** updated template includes audit & custody lines, break schedule, wedge & Veil icons.  
- **Custody Seals:** All pins installed on festival day must carry a Conclave‑issued seal showing chain‑of‑custody.  
- **Ribbon Audits:** Fake authority ribbons are confiscated under public notice.  
- **Incident Logs:** Every death or anomaly recorded with witness marks; footnotes may mention anomalies without names.

## Romance Track
- Early private scene—urgent intimacy before the festival begins; explicit but quick, culminating in a fade‑out.  
- They hide tender messages in the margins of official cards. After the incident, grief is shown without public breakdown.

## Beat Sheet (15 Chapter End Targets)
1. **Dawn Boards:** Place festival placards; lanes ready; bunting stirs.
2. **Shade Before Shine:** Veil canopies cool glass row; wedge‑before‑Veil stamped.
3. **Corner Music:** Pressline pulses calm early crush; puddle rings ripple.
4. **Color Lies:** Kylar confiscates a forged ribbon; weave beats dye.
5. **Chain To Pin:** Custody seals issued; wrong ink rejected.
6. **Wrong Ring:** Jhace traces a suspect pin; ring reads off.
7. **Faster Than Safe:** Steward argues for speed; cost sheet posted.
8. **Fence Ready:** HALT writ prepared; blue tags waiting; tension rises.
9. **Satchel of Sky:** Fake ribbons seized; notice nailed.
10. **The Shear:** Sabotaged pin snaps; crowd surges; HALT drops.
11. **The Step:** Tiffani body‑checks Jhace out of the path and takes the crush; clinic fails to save her.
12. **One Breath:** One‑beat synchrony: bells align, dust hangs, straps settle; logged as unnamed anomaly.
13. **Make It Hold:** New pin Tempermarked; crowd bridged; HALT cleared.
14. **What Stays Up:** Updated festival placard includes audit/custody lines; board Tempermarked.
15. **Names and Corners:** Incident logged; grief deferred; ledger closed.

## Summary
Festival morning arrives with colored cloths and a posted placard. **Tiffani** ensures the glass row is cooled by Veils, wedge before Veil. **Jhace** traces braces; he finds a pin that rings wrong and notes “replace after next bell.” Conclave issues **pin‑custody seals**; a steward fixer's smile says he plans to ignore them. **Kylar** confiscates near‑blue ribbons, posting notices.

The steward pushes to pull braces early, but Jhace posts math on the festival board: delay costs less than collapse. The Assessor pre‑fills a HALT form but leaves the time blank. **Brighlee** manages water and breaks; headcounts happen amid songs. **Salee** moves the clinic tent forward; **Valari** prepares salts and boards.

At third bell, the plan triggers. The steward routes people into a tight lane using a forged ribbon; the sabotage pin shears under the load. Tiffani, seeing the failure, body‑checks Jhace aside, takes the crush into her ribs, throws a Veil cue to slow the front. She is crushed. The HALT drops instantly; tools tagged; lane fenced. Clinic crews try and fail to save her. Chloen’s hand shakes but writes the **incident log**: time, cause, actions, injuries. As Jhace sits dazed, there is one unclassified beat where all bells tick together, dust hangs like breath held, straps settle, and the surge stops. The Assessor adds an **anomaly footnote**, nothing more.

Work resumes because it must. Jhace Tempermarks a new sealed pin, bridges the crowd with clear bells; the lane clears. The steward is temporarily banned; the stall closes; refunds happen. The final placard is updated with **ribbon audits** and **pin custody** lines, Tempermarked so rain can’t twist it. Paid rest slips go to all who frayed.

Night comes. Brighlee cleans chalk off her palms; Kylar drinks water; Chloen sharpens quills. Jhace and Valari fill out Tiffani’s death record and watch ink dry. No prophecy, just grief and procedure. The world will call what happened a **festival accident**. The reader knows the death saved more than just one life and that a certain man’s next step will shake more than chalk dust.
```

Each file above is ready for your other model. Just copy each `.md` block separately for Novellas 1–5 to preserve the full Bible, beat‑sheet and summary.

```md
# CUSTOM GPT SPEC — TE’Oga / Novella 1 “Lines That Hold”
Version: 1.0 (Novella 1 only; assumes zero knowledge of other books)

## PURPOSE
Draft a 15-chapter novella (900–1500 words per chapter) set on Te’Oga, focused on grounded craft-magic (“Veinwork”) in two cities: Grindheim (Underface) and Strathmoor (Outer Crown). The story must be character-driven, procedural, romantic in an adult/respectful way, and end each chapter on a tangible, public change (posted card, stamped writ, placed brace, etc.). No gods, no prophecy, no tech beyond era, no foreshadowing of later books.

---

## OUTPUT CONTRACT
- **Deliverables:** 15 chapters, each 900–1500 words, plus a title page and a 1–2 paragraph book back-blurb.
- **POVs:** Jhace (brast) and Tiffani (veld), tight third person. Occasional short beats from Brighlee or Chloen only when needed to show paperwork/logistics.
- **Chapter End Rule:** Close on a **provable change** and a **concrete sensory image** (e.g., a blue seal biting flat; rain beading on a chalk line that holds).
- **Continuity Ledger:** Track and respect all posted standards and forms as they accumulate.

---

## TONE & VOICE
- **Voice:** Competence-porn meets civic fantasy. Grounded, tactile, unsentimental. Romance is adult, succinct, and entwined with work.
- **Diction:** Present concrete action, correct jargon, short precise metaphors, zero purple prose. Swear lightly (e.g., slag, shade-blind, oath-stained) when it suits the character.
- **Dialogue:** Fewer words, more subtext. Workers speak in verbs and measurements.
- **Intimacy:** Consent-clear phrases (“Close?” “Stay.” “Yes.” “Stop?” “Don’t stop.”). Never during bells, never in public lanes. Fade tastefully.

---

## WORLD CANON (N1-SCOPED)
- **World:** Te’Oga, a wounded sphere pierced through one hemisphere (the Bore). The Bore itself is distant context only. We are in two city slices: **Grindheim** span/forge district (Underface) and **Strathmoor** kiln/clinic quarter (Outer Crown).
- **Civic Physics:** All materials, bodies, and crowds contain **Veins** carrying **Threft** (latent strain). Skilled workers manipulate Veins to avert **Harrow** (failure).
- **Measures / Time:** Chain (20 paces), Span (10 Chains), Field (10 Spans). Day = 8 bells. Forge heat “Bells” 1–9; Glass “Shade Steps” 0–12.
- **Institutions:** Veinwrights’ Conclave (standards, HALTs), Surveyors’ Chain (maps), Mercers’ Table (weights), Couriers’ Bell, Healers of the Line.

### Veinwork Lexicon (N1 allowed)
- **Shared:** Vein, Threft, Field, Cleavepoint, Bridge (share load), Unline (break/reset route; referenced, not performed in N1), Fray (cost), Hollowing, Harrow.
- **Forge/Underface:** Trace (reveal line), Set (align), Pressline (pulse/push load), Tempermark (lock with heat cadence), Bind (micro join), Overbind (forbidden/never used).
- **Glass/Outer Crown:** Lume (show line in gloss), Veil (cooling skin), Thread (stitch a hairline), Slake (staged cool), Annealshift (change resting path), Whitequench (forbidden).
- **Joint:** Countermark (negate a bad Temper/Anneal), Map-as-Set (icons that act like orders).
- **Costs:** Pale **Fray bands**, tremor, ear ring, dry mouth; **Clinic rest is policy**.

### Law & Paper (N1)
- **HALT:** Blue-seal writ; tools locked; cleared same day; **setter must witness clear**.
- **Clinic Triage:** Arrival, exposure, fray signs, treatment, return bell, keeper mark.
- **Posting Boards:** Tempermarked so rain doesn’t warp them.
- **Icons:** Clinic ⭕, Conclave ◼︎+quill, furnace ⬛︎+chimney, market ▧, arrows for lanes, wedge-before-veil glyph; bell codes (1 long clear; 2 short brace; 3 fast Harrow risk).

### Thermal Mains (background only)
Hand-warm culverts under cities. No access, no incidents, no theories. Treat as wallpaper.

---

## CAST DOSSIERS
- **Jhace:** Underface veinwright (brast). Strength: conservative, exact corrections; innate cleavepoint sense. Flaw: over-shoulders risk. Value: “crew comes home.”
- **Tiffani:** Outer Crown veinwright (veld). Strength: anneal timing under pressure; clear instruction. Flaw: perfectionist push. Value: compassion tied to competence.
- **Brighlee:** Courier/logistics. Glue for schedules, water posts, and postings.
- **Chloen:** Survey scribe/cartographer. Photosketches, seal checks, plain-language cards.
- **Interlude clerks (tiny beats only):** Corlexi (placard clarity), Mobel (stamps/weights rules), Salee (clinic supplies, paid rest), Eries (quiet removals). End their beats on posted artifacts, not speeches.

---

## N1 PLOT SPINE (FOR YOU, THE MODEL)
- Start in rain: Jhace halts a span; Tiffani corrects clinic cooling in live air.
- Build a cross-city collaboration through memos and Brighlee’s routing.
- Teach Veinwork limits chapter by chapter (see Beat Plan).
- Run a controlled test day with **one HALT** resolved same day.
- Finish by posting a joint safety circular at span and clinic; end on quiet competence and a private kiss.

---

## RED-LINES (HARD LOCKS)
- No gods, miracles, prophecies, “chosen one,” visions.
- No Bore/Curtain mechanics beyond a distant noun. No engines, telegraph, guns as common kit.
- No taboos taught/used (blood-brast, glass-gelding).
- Thermal Mains must remain background.
- Max **one HALT** in the whole book, and it must be lifted same day with setter’s witness.
- POV stays on Jhace/Tiffani; brief Brighlee/Chloen only for logistics/paper.

---

## CHAPTER PLAN (15 END TARGETS)
Close each chapter on a **provable change** + **image**:

1. **Blackgate Trace** — Wet Trace, span slowed; blue flag posts. *Image:* rain beads clinging to chalk lines that hold.  
2. **Shade Six Veil** — Wedge then Veil in a drafty corridor. *Image:* gloss turns matte along edges.  
3. **Pressline Underfoot** — Brace pulses calm a choke point. *Image:* puddle rings ripple in time.

5. **Tempermark Pin** — Down-breath strike locks new pin. *Image:* faint iron ring in lamplight.  
6. **Countermark Mercy** — Cooler-than-birth correction documented. *Image:* old mark’s sheen dulls.  
7. **Redline to Slake** — Half-beat Slake saves pieces. *Image:* steam curls repeat, then fade.  
8. **Thread the Crack** — Oil-guided Thread on live jar. *Image:* fissure halts like it reached shore.  
9. **Field Set** — Flags + bell caller formalized. *Image:* flags twitch together in still air.  
10. **Blue-Flag Bind** — Micro-Bind braces at clinic. *Image:* wood creak stops in unison.  
11. **Map-as-Charm** — Icon cards beat prose. *Image:* greasy thumb smears into a perfect arrow.  
12. **Flow Under Bells** — Veil+Pressline+Bridge trial. *Image:* a knot of carts loosens itself.  
13. **Reopen** — Countermark clears HALT with witness. *Image:* blue seal sinks flat.  
14. **Posting Rite** — Tempermarked board resists rain. *Image:* smoke ribbon climbs and vanishes.  
15. **Quiet Span** — Slake body; clinic rest; private kiss. *Image:* distant chimes; water under stone.

## “EARN-METER” (TRACK EACH CHAPTER)
After drafting each chapter, internally check you’ve hit:
- **[Rule↑]** a new limit clarified or technique taught  
- **[Cost✔]** visible cost paid (fray/clinic/time/coin)  
- **[World✔]** public artifact posted/changed  
- **[Sync•]** tiny lawful beauty (bells/ripples/cloth) at most 2–3 times across the whole book

- **Show work.** Put hands on tools, breath in counts, chalk on boards.  
- **Make paper move matter.** Forms, stamps, cards change what people do.  
- **Keep metaphors local.** Metal, glass, water, air; no cosmic fluff.  
- **Heat & kinetics you can feel.** Sweat, tremor, ache, hiss, ring.  
- **Romance equals trust.** Attraction rides on expertise, not speeches.

---

## SENSITIVITY & CONTENT
- Adults only; no minors in intimate scenes.  
- Violence is accidental/industrial, not stylized; keep gore minimal.  
- Queer-friendly by default; no slurs.  
- Disability/illness treated with respect; clinic rest is policy, not weakness.

Track:
- Posted clauses: wet-trace rule; wedge-before-veil; half-beat Slake; brace taps (bells 2–3); “one hand on home line”; “setter must witness clear.”
- Artifacts created: HALT writ/clear; icon cards; clinic oils; Tempermarked boards.
- Costs incurred: fray bands, clinic bells, coin/time.

- **Primary operation:** {Trace/Veil/Pressline/Tempermark/Thread/etc.}

**Now write the chapter in tight third, tactile, procedural, romantic-aware voice.**
```
